RaceName: British Stallions Studs EBF Nursery Handicap (A Hamilton Park 2-Y-O Series Qualifier)
Spotlight: Never involved from the widest draw at Chester in May but he'd run two good races previously, on Musselburgh debut (5f, good; 66-1) then looked the likely winner over slightly further at Chester before run down late; the stiff finish on this track should see him to good effect; handicap debut.

RaceName: British Stallions Studs EBF Nursery Handicap (A Hamilton Park 2-Y-O Series Qualifier)
Spotlight: Has improved with each start, running creditably in a valuable seller at Musselburgh on second start, then coming clear in Ffos Las maiden (5f, soft) four weeks ago, where the stiffer test at the trip, soft ground and possibly first-time cheekpieces may all have played a part; this provides a different test but she may be up to it.

RaceName: British Stallions Studs EBF Nursery Handicap (A Hamilton Park 2-Y-O Series Qualifier)
Spotlight: Has run to a similar level in four starts since her debut, coming home strongly when winning a C&D maiden three weeks ago (good to firm; runner-up went close in a handicap next time); mark adjusted by 4lb since but in the thick of it now handicapping.

RaceName: British Stallions Studs EBF Nursery Handicap (A Hamilton Park 2-Y-O Series Qualifier)
Spotlight: Has ability but he was difficult to keep on the straight and narrow when making his handicap debut at Yarmouth (5f, good to firm) last week and was eased inside the final furlong; queried now with first-time visor replaced with cheekpieces.

RaceName: Best Ticket Deals Online @thirskraces Book Now Restricted Maiden Fillies' Stakes (Bands C/D) (GBB)
Spotlight: Hooded for her first three starts; third at Newcastle (6f, AW) in two starts last year and also ran well in two starts this term (turf/AW), most recently when beaten a neck at Southwell (hood removed) 11 days ago on her first try at this trip; returns to turf and she has strong claims.

RaceName: Best Ticket Deals Online @thirskraces Book Now Restricted Maiden Fillies' Stakes (Bands C/D) (GBB)
Spotlight: Ran well when runner-up at Southwell (5f, AW) on her final start for Grant Tuer but she was tailed off in a handicap at Leicester (6f, good to firm) on her debut for this stable; steps up in trip with a bit to prove.

RaceName: Best Ticket Deals Online @thirskraces Book Now Restricted Maiden Fillies' Stakes (Bands C/D) (GBB)
Spotlight: Promising third of eight on her debut at Yarmouth (7f, good to soft) behind a subsequent Group 3 winner last summer but she was below that level on her next two starts, including when last of four at Epsom last August on her final start for Eve Johnson Houghton; absent since and the market should be informative on her stable debut.

RaceName: togetheragainfestival.co.uk Maiden Fillies' Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: 125-1 when offering little short-term encouragement on last month's debut (7.3f, good).

RaceName: togetheragainfestival.co.uk Maiden Fillies' Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Threatened on occasions as a 2yo and also excelled herself when a close fifth in a Group 3 in France (1m, very soft) last October; began her 3yo campaign with two solid efforts but she was well beaten at Royal Ascot four weeks ago; hood now tried and a tongue tie returns; won't find a better chance to break her duck.

RaceName: togetheragainfestival.co.uk Maiden Fillies' Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Similar form in her three starts (7f-1m, good or faster), going close at this track in May and turning in another solid effort when fifth at Doncaster last month; returning to 1m should suit and she won't need much improvement for the addition of a hood.

RaceName: Wellman Cars Hamilton Handicap
Spotlight: Resumed winning ways at Wolverhampton (5f) in May and comes here on the back of a solid third of 13 in 6f Ayr handicap ten days ago; must enter calculations eased 1lb in his bid for a first turf victory.

RaceName: Wellman Cars Hamilton Handicap
Spotlight: Enjoying a fine season and his fifth win (including over C&D) of the campaign came at Wolverhampton (7f) two days ago when completing a quick hat-trick; leading claims turned out under a 5lb penalty.

RaceName: Wellman Cars Hamilton Handicap
Spotlight: Low-mileage sort who posted easily his best effort for new yard with second in 5f Ayr handicap last month; needs considering off an unchanged mark.

RaceName: Wellman Cars Hamilton Handicap
Spotlight: Gained a second win of 2026 at Musselburgh (5f) in June; only seventh of 11 in 5f Ayr handicap three days ago so she needs to bounce back.

RaceName: Wellman Cars Hamilton Handicap
OwnerName: N Bradley Racing 29 & South Bank Racing
Spotlight: Runner-up in C&D novice in May but she has failed to go on sent handicapping, last of ten at Musselburgh (5f) last month;  cheekpieces are reached for now.

RaceName: Wellman Cars Hamilton Handicap
OwnerName: Let's Be Lucky Racing 38 & Steven Wilson
Spotlight: 0-20 and he arrives below par, just fifth of ten in 5f Musselburgh handicap last month; others appeal more.

RaceName: Wellman Cars Hamilton Handicap
Spotlight: Remains winless and she beat one over C&D five days ago; more is required if she's to gain a breakthrough win at the 12th attempt.

RaceName: Wellman Cars Hamilton Handicap
Spotlight: Took this in 2025 but winless since and just a fair fifth of eight over C&D five days ago; this veteran needs to take a step forward.

RaceName: By Chen Presidential Apprentice Handicap
Spotlight: Won two nurseries at Beverley over 7.5f last August (both on good to firm) but she struggled in a competitive sales race at Doncaster (6.5f, good to soft) in September when last seen; returns off a mark 8lb higher than for that latest success but she can't be dismissed back in a handicap.

RaceName: By Chen Presidential Apprentice Handicap
Spotlight: Off the mark at the ninth attempt when winning at Carlisle (7f, good) last month and ran another good race when bumping into an improver at Beverley (8.5f, good to firm) 12 days ago; contender off the same mark.

RaceName: By Chen Presidential Apprentice Handicap
Spotlight: Promising third at Lingfield (7f, AW) in March but he was well beaten on his other two outings for Richard Hughes, and he was beaten a long way on his stable/handicap debut at Carlisle (1m1f, good to soft; first-time cheekpieces) last month; dropped 5lb.

RaceName: By Chen Presidential Apprentice Handicap
Spotlight: Well held on her debut at Newcastle (7f, AW) in April but she has taken a small step forward in each of her two starts since; kept on into fourth at Thirsk (7f, good) last month and could be suited by this step up to 1m on her handicap debut.

RaceName: By Chen Presidential Apprentice Handicap
Spotlight: Posted an improved effort when runner-up at Ripon (1m, good) last month and went one better at Beverley (8.5f, good to firm; made all) two weeks ago, form that has since been franked by the second; cheekpieces now go on and he should give it a good go from the front.

RaceName: LSL Racing Horse Sales Syndication Leasing Apprentice Handicap
Spotlight: Veteran who arrives in top form, winning 6f handicaps here and at Ffos Las last month (both good ground); this represents an ease in grade but neither the drop in trip nor the forecast faster ground are positives.

RaceName: LSL Racing Horse Sales Syndication Leasing Apprentice Handicap
Spotlight: Back on the up following a wind op in May, placed at Bath and over C&D before making all in a small-field handicap at Lingfield (6f, good to firm) 19 days ago; a 4lb rise asks more of her but she should give another good account; stable also run So Smart.

RaceName: LSL Racing Horse Sales Syndication Leasing Apprentice Handicap
Spotlight: C&D winner; should be well treated now but that has been the case for a while and he keeps finding one or two too strong; conditions no problem and likely to give his running again; stable also run Savannah Smiles.

RaceName: LSL Racing Horse Sales Syndication Leasing Apprentice Handicap
Spotlight: 0-12 but he has been threatening for Tony Carroll this year, most recently finishing second in a C&D handicap 19 days ago; should get a good pace to aim at and unlikely to be far away again.

RaceName: LSL Racing Horse Sales Syndication Leasing Apprentice Handicap
Spotlight: He was returning from 736 days on the sidelines when third of eight in a 0-50 classified event over C&D (good to firm) two weeks ago; tired in the closing stages on that occasion and he should last longer with the run behind him; open to improvement.

RaceName: Alex McWilliams Memorial Celebrations Restricted Novice Stakes (Bands B/C/D) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Well held in a pair of 7f novices at Newcastle and Carlisle eight months apart; significantly more is required.

RaceName: Alex McWilliams Memorial Celebrations Restricted Novice Stakes (Bands B/C/D) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Victor Ludorum gelding; half-brother to 7f 2yo winner Brastias (RPR 72); dam 1m-1m6f maiden (RPR 66), sister to 1m4f-15.5f winner Theatro; has had wind surgery ahead of his debut but in good hands and needs considering.

RaceName: Alex McWilliams Memorial Celebrations Restricted Novice Stakes (Bands B/C/D) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Has shown fair form when runner-up on all four starts, most recently in 1m2f novice at Doncaster last month; since gelded and this son of Blue Point is the one to beat with cheekpieces added.

RaceName: Alex McWilliams Memorial Celebrations Restricted Novice Stakes (Bands B/C/D) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Sent off 80-1 but shaped well when fourth of ten in Doncaster maiden (1m, soft) on his debut in September; off since but this Time Test gelding remains with potential.

RaceName: Alex McWilliams Memorial Celebrations Restricted Novice Stakes (Bands B/C/D) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: 66-1, hinted at promise with a staying-on fourth of seven in maiden here (1m1f, good to firm) on his debut three weeks ago; this son of Churchill can do better in the longer term.

RaceName: Alex McWilliams Memorial Celebrations Restricted Novice Stakes (Bands B/C/D) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: 40-1 and green when a never-dangerous sixth of eight in maiden at Ayr (6f, soft) on her debut last month; needs to take a big step forward.

RaceName: Sell Your Business With Anderson Barrowcliff Handicap
Spotlight: Ended 2025 with two wins on the AW at Newcastle and reappeared with a good fourth over this C&D in May; failed to build on that at York (5f, good) last month but now drops in grade.

RaceName: Sell Your Business With Anderson Barrowcliff Handicap
OwnerName: Grange Park Racing Xviii And Ruth Carr
Spotlight: Winless since her debut but she has been runner-up on three occasions, including at Bath (5f, firm) in April; below that level on her final three starts for the Newland/Insole yard and makes her stable debut having been bought for 12,000gns.

RaceName: Sell Your Business With Anderson Barrowcliff Handicap
Spotlight: Both wins have been at Ripon over 6f, the most recent on good to soft in April; reported to have bled from the nose when failing to beat a rival at York (5f, good) in first-time cheekpieces (discarded) last month and needs to bounce back.

RaceName: Sell Your Business With Anderson Barrowcliff Handicap
Spotlight: Doubled his tally when winning at Ripon (6f, good) in May and ran well when third here over 6f two weeks ago; cheekpieces are now added but he will likely need them to go a good gallop dropped back in trip.

RaceName: Sell Your Business With Anderson Barrowcliff Handicap
OwnerName: J Raybould N Skinner D Jenkins
Spotlight: Had a visor on for the first time when winning at Newcastle (5f, AW) in May but he's been well beaten in both starts since, including here over 6f last time; headgear is now discarded.

RaceName: Sell Your Business With Anderson Barrowcliff Handicap
Spotlight: 0-6 but he went close in a novice at Catterick (5f, good to firm; first-time tongue-tie) in May; behind a few of these at York last month and was beaten 6l when runner-up at Redcar four weeks ago; dropped 3lb.

RaceName: Sell Your Business With Anderson Barrowcliff Handicap
Spotlight: Won a fillies' novice at Musselburgh (5f, soft) on her final start for Kevin Philippart de Foy and has posted some respectable efforts for this stable, including when fourth at Newcastle three weeks ago; place claims.

RaceName: Sell Your Business With Anderson Barrowcliff Handicap
Spotlight: Sole win came over this C&D last May on his second start and he ran respectably when sixth of 12 here in May; however, he's run below that level in two starts since and needs to bounce back returned to this track.

RaceName: Sell Your Business With Anderson Barrowcliff Handicap
Spotlight: Won at Musselbugh (5f, good) last August but he's struggled on his last two outings, including on his reappearance at Leicester (5f, good) last month following a wind operation; best watched.

RaceName: EBF / UB40 ft. Ali Campbell 24th July Restricted Novice (Bands B/C/D) (EBF Restricted Qual') (GBB)
Spotlight: Finished in midfield on his debut at Nottingham (5f, good to soft) last month; better form when second of eight at Windsor (5f, good to firm) 16 days later; holds leading form claims while retaining the potential for further progress.

RaceName: EBF / UB40 ft. Ali Campbell 24th July Restricted Novice (Bands B/C/D) (EBF Restricted Qual') (GBB)
Spotlight: 65,000euros yearling; fourth foal; brother to winners Nemonte (6f/7.4f 2yo; RPR 83) and Dahlia Noir (1m; 79); dam Listed-placed 1m-12.5f winner (92), half-sister to 1m2f 2yo Group 1 winner Tennessee Stud; bred to need a bit more of a test than this but he is starting out at a realistic level; market should be instructive.

RaceName: EBF / UB40 ft. Ali Campbell 24th July Restricted Novice (Bands B/C/D) (EBF Restricted Qual') (GBB)
Spotlight: 28-1 for both starts, running to a similar modest RPR in the process; hopes appear to rest on the drop to 5f and switch to faster ground sparking some improvement.

RaceName: EBF / UB40 ft. Ali Campbell 24th July Restricted Novice (Bands B/C/D) (EBF Restricted Qual') (GBB)
Spotlight: Dropped away to finish seventh of eight (Blondie Larry second) in a 5f novice at Windsor 19 days ago; open to improvement but likely a longer-term prospect.

RaceName: EBF / UB40 ft. Ali Campbell 24th July Restricted Novice (Bands B/C/D) (EBF Restricted Qual') (GBB)
Spotlight: Modest form in two 5f runs last month (gelded after debut, a race for which he was sent off the 15-8 favourite); still early days but nurseries may provide richer pickings.

RaceName: EBF / UB40 ft. Ali Campbell 24th July Restricted Novice (Bands B/C/D) (EBF Restricted Qual') (GBB)
Spotlight: Promising start to her career when second of eight in an early-season fillies' maiden at Wolverhampton (5f, AW) 101 days ago; the winner and third from that race are both decent performers and she looks a likely type on her return from a break.

RaceName: EBF / UB40 ft. Ali Campbell 24th July Restricted Novice (Bands B/C/D) (EBF Restricted Qual') (GBB)
Spotlight: Held back by inexperience in her two runs so far, showing better form at the second attempt when sixth of ten at Newbury (6f, good to firm) 23 days ago; needs more to feature but that isn't out of the question.

RaceName: EBF / UB40 ft. Ali Campbell 24th July Restricted Novice (Bands B/C/D) (EBF Restricted Qual') (GBB)
Spotlight: Poor form in fast-ground maidens at Windsor (5f) and here (7f) this summer; can't be recommended.

RaceName: EBF / UB40 ft. Ali Campbell 24th July Restricted Novice (Bands B/C/D) (EBF Restricted Qual') (GBB)
Spotlight: Shaped well when fourth over C&D (soft) on debut last month; sent off the 2-1 favourite for a fillies' novice at Newbury (6f, good to firm) on the strength of it but she dropped away to finish eighth of the ten runners (jockey reported the filly boiled over in the preliminaries); now wears a hood; can do better.

RaceName: Wellman Cars Private Hire Almada Mile Handicap
Spotlight: High-class handicap form at 6f to 1m on a range of surfaces and he was up against it when second to a progressive winner in this race last year; has since won two handicaps at Meydan for new yard and perhaps felt the lack of a recent run when mid-division in the Buckingham Palace at Royal Ascot last month; leading player.

RaceName: Wellman Cars Private Hire Almada Mile Handicap
Spotlight: A superstar down the years and he's quite well treated again now but all his handicap wins at 7f and 1m have been on downhill tracks on good to soft or slower ground; better days ahead once the rains come.

RaceName: Wellman Cars Private Hire Almada Mile Handicap
Spotlight: Sole French win on heavy ground but he's excelled on good and quicker for new yard, having far too much power for his rivals when making all here (about 1m1f) in May and doing really well to be third (demoted to fifth) in a major York handicap (extended 1m2f) on Saturday; needs a strong pace back at this trip which he may well set himself; good claims.

RaceName: Wellman Cars Private Hire Almada Mile Handicap
Spotlight: Justified short odds in 6f Doncaster maiden on sole 2yo start; much better form on reappearance at Leicester (7f, good) in April, looking strong in the closing stages against rivals who have given the form a weighty look since; has to prove stamina over the new trip, and his pedigree is more about sprinting on the dam's side, but still intriguing on handicap debut if ground is thought suitable (missed recent intended run on good to firm).

RaceName: Wellman Cars Private Hire Almada Mile Handicap
Spotlight: Two 6f AW wins as a juvenile and he nearly pulled off a step up in trip when going down narrowly at Chester (extended 7f, good) in May; had no chance of getting involved after getting badly hampered on the rail at Epsom in June; gelded since; open to some improvement but he's another trying a new trip.

RaceName: Don't Miss Summer Family Days @thirskraces Classified Stakes
Spotlight: Six-time AW winner for Alice Haynes, the most recent coming at Chelmsford last August; 5lb lower than for that success but he's been well held in four starts since joining this stable.

RaceName: Don't Miss Summer Family Days @thirskraces Classified Stakes
Spotlight: Struggled in novice/maiden company on her first three starts and it was the same story in a classified event at Leicester (7f, good; first-time cheekpieces) last month; blinkers need to make a difference.

RaceName: Don't Miss Summer Family Days @thirskraces Classified Stakes
Spotlight: Ex-Irish maiden who was runner-up at Gowran Park (1m, good to yielding) in May but below that level in both starts since, including when last of 14 on her yard debut at Beverley (8.5f, good) a month ago.

RaceName: Don't Miss Summer Family Days @thirskraces Classified Stakes
OwnerName: Mrs Sally Doyle And Mrs Ruth Carr
Spotlight: C&D winner who was runner-up over 8.5f at Beverley in April and May; below his best over that same C&D last month but has good claims on his first try in classified company.

RaceName: Don't Miss Summer Family Days @thirskraces Classified Stakes
OwnerName: Dixon, Wylam, M Baldry, J&s Harrod
Spotlight: Won twice over 1m at Southwell last year; struggled on her first four outings this term but returned to some form when fourth of 18 at Doncaster (1m, good to soft) last month; needs to build on that.

RaceName: Don't Miss Summer Family Days @thirskraces Classified Stakes
Spotlight: Three-time winner, the most recent in a classified at Newcastle (1m, AW) in February; ran well there three weeks ago and has to be considered returned to this level.

RaceName: Don't Miss Summer Family Days @thirskraces Classified Stakes
Spotlight: Dual C&D winner; got up late to win a handicap at Beverley (7.5f, good) last month and ran another respectable race when fourth in a classified event at Ayr (1m; Park Lane Penny was second) 11 days ago; claims.

RaceName: Don't Miss Summer Family Days @thirskraces Classified Stakes
Spotlight: The most recent of her five wins came at Catterick last July; third there over 7f in May but she's struggled in three outings since, including back at Catterick eight days ago; others have stronger claims.

RaceName: Don't Miss Summer Family Days @thirskraces Classified Stakes
Spotlight: Well beaten on his first three starts (1m/1m4f) but he was beaten only 3l when sixth of ten on his handicap debut at Wetherby (1m2f, good to firm) last month; should have more to offer but didn't look to be crying out for this drop back in trip.

RaceName: Don't Miss Summer Family Days @thirskraces Classified Stakes
Spotlight: Struggled on her first three starts but posted an improved effort when narrowly beaten in a classified event at Ayr (1m, good) 11 days ago; showed signs of inexperience that day (hung left) and looks capable of going one better.

RaceName: Don't Miss Summer Family Days @thirskraces Classified Stakes
Spotlight: Eight-race maiden who failed to improved for the addition of a tongue-tie (retained) at Ayr (1m, soft) last month; better needed.

RaceName: Don't Miss Summer Family Days @thirskraces Classified Stakes
OwnerName: D Bishop P Robinson S Bland T Coyle
Spotlight: Eight-race maiden but he did run one of his better races when third of seven at Beverley (8.5f, good to firm; first-time blinkers) two weeks ago; claims if backing that up in a switch of headgear.

RaceName: Don't Miss Summer Family Days @thirskraces Classified Stakes
Spotlight: Off the mark at the 12th attempt when beating seven rivals in a handicap at Ripon (1m, good) four weeks ago; that was her first go over 1m and she drops into a classified event with solid claims.

RaceName: Fairplay Daily Price Boosts Handicap
Spotlight: 7f AW win for Denis Quinn last season; running well for his new trainer too, most recently finishing a neck second at Epsom (good to firm) last week; runs off the same mark (1lb well in) and he is respected, for all his stamina for 1m has to be conclusively proven.

RaceName: Fairplay Daily Price Boosts Handicap
Spotlight: Ran his best race for this yard when keeping on for third in a C&D handicap 19 days ago; now has something to build on and Billy Loughnane a positive booking for the stable.

RaceName: Fairplay Daily Price Boosts Handicap
Spotlight: Four-time AW winner who ran her best race on turf when second of seven, beaten a neck, at Nottingham (8.3f, good) in May; failed to back it up at Yarmouth (1m2f, good to firm; cheekpieces rather than visor) four weeks ago; this trip should be more suitable.

RaceName: Fairplay Daily Price Boosts Handicap
Spotlight: Well placed to rattle off a fast-ground hat-trick last summer (7f/1m); this campaign has been harder work but he fared a bit better last time, is down to a winning mark and now wears cheekpieces for the first time; not solid, but a revival wouldn't be a complete bolt from the blue.

RaceName: Fairplay Daily Price Boosts Handicap
Spotlight: Picked up a couple of Class 6 events at Bath this summer and he wasn't beaten far in his hat-trick bid at Ffos Las (7.3f, good) 16 days ago; another 2lb higher today but this C&D winner should give it another good go.

RaceName: Fairplay Daily Price Boosts Handicap
Spotlight: 8lb better off with Dappled Light for a half-length defeat at Bath (1m, good to firm) in May; won a small-field handicap back at Bath four days later but two runs in Class 5 events since haven't been so good; others appeal more.

RaceName: Every Race Live On Racing TV Handicap
Spotlight: A fair 7f winner for John and Thady Gosden last summer; has steadily found his form for current yard, runner-up in five-runner event over C&D five days ago; player off an unchanged mark.

RaceName: Every Race Live On Racing TV Handicap
Spotlight: Gained a first win of 2026 in 1m handicap at Carlisle 12 days ago, getting up late to deny Inspired having been set a lot to do; bagged a remarkable seven successes (including here) last season so merits serious consideration now she's up and running this time around.

RaceName: Every Race Live On Racing TV Handicap
OwnerName: Fine Claret Racing,Mrs Burke & C Wood
Spotlight: Scored at Newcastle (1m) in December and has proved reliable without adding to his tally this season, a clear second of seven to Jannas Journey in 1m Carlisle handicap 12 days ago; not taken lightly despite a 2lb weights rise.

RaceName: Every Race Live On Racing TV Handicap
Spotlight: A fair 1m scorer for Andrew Balding in 2024; off 22 months but quickly back on track with second of ten in 1m2f handicap at Ayr 11 days ago; needs considering off an unchanged mark.

RaceName: Every Race Live On Racing TV Handicap
Spotlight: Ex-German 1m1f winner; gained a first win for current yard over C&D in May and not discredited when third of five (Kaleido runner-up) over C&D five days ago; not out of things with relatively few miles still on the clock.

RaceName: Meijer Celebration Cup Handicap
Spotlight: Ten-race maiden but he went close at Chepstow (6f, good to soft) on his final start for Adrian Wintle, and ran well at Wetherby (5.5f, good to firm; didn't get the clearest run) last month on his stable debut; this trip will be no issue and he's considered.

RaceName: Meijer Celebration Cup Handicap
Spotlight: Course winner last April when trained by Declan Carroll; runner-up in two of his six starts since joining this stable and shaped as if still in form when sixth of eight at Catterick (7f, good to firm) 24 days ago; can go well if things drop right (usually held up).

RaceName: Meijer Celebration Cup Handicap
Spotlight: Won four times last year, the most recent coming at Catterick off a 9lb higher mark than today's; her last two efforts have been solid and she's an each-way contender.

RaceName: Meijer Celebration Cup Handicap
Spotlight: Lightly-raced 6yo mare who has hinted at ability in two of her three starts (6f/1m; soft/good to firm) but she was still well beaten here two weeks ago; drops back in trip on her handicap debut.

RaceName: Meijer Celebration Cup Handicap
Spotlight: Nine-race maiden but he's been shaping quite well in recent starts, including when fourth of 18 here last time over 6f, doing best of those drawn low; looks worth another try over this longer trip.

RaceName: Meijer Celebration Cup Handicap
Spotlight: Won a handicap at Wolverhampton (6f, AW) in February and was runner-up there over 5f on his next start; below his best on turf on his last two outings and has a bit to prove on this surface on his first try over 7f.

RaceName: Meijer Celebration Cup Handicap
Spotlight: Yet to win in handicap company but she's been placed on her last two outings, most recently at Carlisle (1m, good) 12 days ago; drops back in trip with each-way claims.

RaceName: Meijer Celebration Cup Handicap
Spotlight: Eight-race maiden whose best effort came when a 1l third of nine at Doncaster (7f, good to firm; first-time tongue-tie) in May; had cheekpieces on for the first time (quickly discarded) when a fair third of six at Beverley (7.5f, good to firm) 12 days ago.

RaceName: Meijer Celebration Cup Handicap
Spotlight: Beaten 13l when sixth of 12 on her debut at Newcastle (1m, AW) in April and has been well beaten in both starts since (7.5f/7f); makes her handicap debut with better needed.

RaceName: Meijer Celebration Cup Handicap
Spotlight: Seven-race maiden who was held at Carlisle (6f, soft) last month following a wind operation; others have stronger claims.

RaceName: Irish EBF Median Sires Series Maiden (IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: Failed to sell at 50,000gns as a yearling; second foal; half-brother by Pinatubo to a 5f/6f winner in Britain and Hong Kong; dam 1m AW 2yo winner.

RaceName: Irish EBF Median Sires Series Maiden (IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: 65,000gns yearling; Sergei Prokofiev colt is the first foal out of an unraced half-sister to French 6f 2yo Listed winner Royal Grey, out of 6f 2yo Group 2 winner; trainer introduced a smart juvenile at this venue last week.

RaceName: Irish EBF Median Sires Series Maiden (IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: E25,000 yearling; third foal; half-brother by Ardad to two winners; dam 6f winner, half-sister to 6f Listed winner Bounty Box; one of two here for the stable, a senior jockey aboard.

RaceName: Irish EBF Median Sires Series Maiden (IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: 50,000gns yearling; sixth foal; half-brother by the speedy Caturra to three winners; dam Listed-placed 6f 2yo winner, half-sister to 6f 2yo Listed winner System.

RaceName: Irish EBF Median Sires Series Maiden (IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: 125,000gns yearling; sixth foal; half-sister by top-class juvenile sprinter Perfect Power to useful Group-placed winner Thunder Of Niagara, and to a minor 1m winner; dam placed, half-sister to US 1m4f Grade 3 winner Dress Rehearsal.

RaceName: Irish EBF Median Sires Series Maiden (IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: E16,000 Y, £45,000 2yo; third foal; half-sister to Polish 1m winner Cotai Spirit; dam placed 7f AW 2yo, half-sister to 6f 2yo winner Lajooje, out of 7f/1m Listed winning half-sister to 5f Group 1 winner Equiano.

RaceName: Irish EBF Median Sires Series Maiden (IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: Chased home a strongly-fancied rival from the Ger Lyons stable at Fairyhouse last week; should go one better now, since the winner had two runs under her belt and is reportedly a candidate for the valuable Ballyhane Stakes; stable maintaining terrific form.

RaceName: Irish EBF Median Sires Series Maiden (IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: Fair first effort at Roscommon with this rider aboard; stable's newcomer has a senior rider aboard; will probably need a handicap mark.

RaceName: Irish EBF Median Sires Series Maiden (IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: (E6,000 vendor Y); second foal; half-sister to 6f 2yo winner Nakamura; unraced dam closely related to 5f-1m winner Bernie's Boy, half-sister to useful 5f-7f winner Gentlemen; hooded for debut; in good hands but best watched.

RaceName: Irish EBF Median Sires Series Maiden (IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: Made late headway from an unpromising position at Bellewstown; longer trip should suit, but needs to find considerable improvement.

RaceName: Irish EBF Median Sires Series Maiden (IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: Showed the benefit of an initial run (seventh of ten at this venue) when third at Fairyhouse; this looks a stronger race, but could challenge for a share of the money.

RaceName: Irish EBF Median Sires Series Maiden (IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: Gave problems at the start and never showed with a chance first time out at Cork.

RaceName: Bet Now With fairplaybet.co.uk Handicap
Spotlight: Made all to win a 6f handicap at Wolverhampton nine days ago; had form at this trip in Ireland and he comes here in preference to an intended Wolverhampton run on Tuesday; suspicion remains that he's likely to prove a cut above this level.

RaceName: Bet Now With fairplaybet.co.uk Handicap
Spotlight: Course winner (6f) who won decisively on AW (7f) in November off a 3lb higher mark; recent return to action should have sharpened him up; one of two strong contenders for his trainer.

RaceName: Bet Now With fairplaybet.co.uk Handicap
Spotlight: Promising 2yo for Owen Burrows (RPR 83); 0-20 in Ireland for Denis Hogan, with 2026 offering no sign of a revival in his fortunes; starts out for a new yard off a lowly mark so worth a precautionary market check.

RaceName: Bet Now With fairplaybet.co.uk Handicap
Spotlight: Has three course wins over 1m but has found it tougher going this year; slowly easing in the weights but dropping back to 7f is not enough to tempt.

RaceName: Bet Now With fairplaybet.co.uk Handicap
Spotlight: Off the mark at the 31st attempt when winning a 6f handicap here (soft) last month; not quite so good twice since but he is still capable of a prominent showing.

RaceName: Bet Now With fairplaybet.co.uk Handicap
OwnerName: Mrs S M Maine & Roger Gould
Spotlight: Four of his seven wins have come over C&D; successful off this mark at Wolverhampton in March but not in the same form in four runs since; others look more persuasive.

RaceName: Bet Now With fairplaybet.co.uk Handicap
Spotlight: Narrow winner of a 6f handicap here (good to soft) in May and that has been followed by three similar efforts in defeat; creeping up the weights so she will need a bit more now back up to 7f.

RaceName: Bet Now With fairplaybet.co.uk Handicap
Spotlight: Strong at the finish when winning a C&D handicap last May off 1lb higher; retains all his ability, in the frame on his last two start, and going back over this trip will suit; one of two runners for the stable.

RaceName: Bet Now With fairplaybet.co.uk Handicap
Spotlight: Infrequent winner; did score over C&D last July off a 3lb higher mark though and his latest Ffos Las second (7.3f, good) was a sound run; each-way shout.

RaceName: Bet Now With fairplaybet.co.uk Handicap
Spotlight: In good form over 1m last month, winning here (soft) before solid placed efforts both here and at Ffos Las; drops back to 7f and probably needs to improve for it.

RaceName: Bet Now With fairplaybet.co.uk Handicap
Spotlight: He ran his best race for some time when second of five in a handicap over C&D (good to firm) last week; fair fourth at Bath on Wednesday but others appeal more for win purposes.

RaceName: Bet Now With fairplaybet.co.uk Handicap
Spotlight: Ended a run of finishing last when seventh of 11 at Brighton nine days ago; easy enough to swerve.

RaceName: INCO Broking Maiden Hurdle
Spotlight: Runner up on last two starts over hurdles; rated 117 and leading claims on paper but disappointing effort when beaten at short odds in 1m4f Limerick claimer on the Flat on latest, hanging to the right.

RaceName: INCO Broking Maiden Hurdle
Spotlight: Affinisea gelding; 1-4 in point-to-points but the form of his win hasn't worked out; beaten 26l when second of two finishers in winners' race at Necarne latest; more needed on belated Rules' debut.

RaceName: INCO Broking Maiden Hurdle
Spotlight: Very promising start when a close third in valuable Fairyhouse bumper (2m, sft-hvy) in April 2025; not seen again until a 4.5l fourth of five back at Fairyhouse (2m, yielding) in January; now steps up in trip for hurdling debut after another absence with Townend up; could go well.

RaceName: INCO Broking Maiden Hurdle
Spotlight: Soundly beaten in a Kilbeggan bumper on debut a month ago when a 12-1 shot; likely best watched upped in trip for hurdling bow.

RaceName: INCO Broking Maiden Hurdle
Spotlight: 0-15 over hurdles and rated 100; has a few placed efforts to his name in maidens, but pulled up at Wexford on latest and needs to bounce back.

RaceName: INCO Broking Maiden Hurdle
Spotlight: Castle Du Berlais gelding; unsold as 3yo; half-brother to French 2m-2m5f hurdle/chase winner Joly Risk; dam maiden half-sister to 1m7f-2m5f chase winner Joly Maker; best watched on debut unless the market speaks.

RaceName: INCO Broking Maiden Hurdle
OwnerName: Ms P Twiss & J Twiss & M O'Sullivan & S Nevin Twiss
Spotlight: Flat maiden placed on all five starts over hurdles at 2m-2m1f, earning a rating of 118; a shade unlucky on the Flat at Limerick last time; beaten 5.5l when third of 14 on penultimate in a maiden hurdle at Cork; longer trip and good ground are queries; tongue tie goes on.

RaceName: INCO Broking Maiden Hurdle
Spotlight: Remote third in a Kilbeggan bumper on debut for Willie Mullins; poor form so far for this yard, including in maiden hurdles the last twice; hard to fancy.

RaceName: INCO Broking Maiden Hurdle
Spotlight: Berkshire mare was pulled up and beaten 21l in two point-to-points; tongue tie is applied for rules debut; best watched.

RaceName: cubeglassladiesnight@hamiltonpark Handicap
Spotlight: Won this race in 2022 and finished runner-up in two of three subsequent runnings; looked to be running into form again when fourth of eight at Catterick (7f, good to firm) 24 days ago and a very good run is likely.

RaceName: cubeglassladiesnight@hamiltonpark Handicap
Spotlight: Has an ordinary strike-rate overall but he broke his turf duck over C&D (good to soft; acts well on good to firm) in June and nearly always runs well in sprints on this track; one of the better options.

RaceName: cubeglassladiesnight@hamiltonpark Handicap
OwnerName: Ursa Major Racing Ltd And Partner
Spotlight: Not in the same form as he was in May and June last year but he's on a lowly mark now and, after being pushed along from some way out, he did stick well to the task when fourth over C&D (good to firm) recently; form case to be made down in grade.

RaceName: cubeglassladiesnight@hamiltonpark Handicap
Spotlight: All three turf wins at 5f, including on this course, but she has won over this trip on the AW and ran well enough over 6f at Pontefract (good) recently to expect her to be on the premises.

RaceName: cubeglassladiesnight@hamiltonpark Handicap
OwnerName: Ursa Major Racing Ltd And Partner
Spotlight: Had a good run of form in early summer last year, getting off the mark at Redcar (6f, good to firm), but rather up and down since and she found little at Ayr ten days ago; hard to rule out if back to best, though.

RaceName: cubeglassladiesnight@hamiltonpark Handicap
Spotlight: Winning return from a long absence in early 2025 but she's finding it hard to sustain her effort in sprints on AW and turf this year, even allowing for finishing second of eight on Newcastle AW (5f) in June; headgear left off; up against it.

RaceName: cubeglassladiesnight@hamiltonpark Handicap
Spotlight: Minor form in a handful of starts, including at classified level and in handicaps, but this is only her third run for Jim Goldie and the return to 6f could suit on second run since wind surgery; may yet improve; cheekpieces fitted.

RaceName: Thirsk Sky Bet Summer Festival Day Friday 24th July Handicap
Spotlight: A three-time 7f/1m winner in 2025, including over this C&D; mainly below his best this season but he didn't run too badly when sixth at Beverley (7.5f, good to firm) 12 days ago; on a handy mark and now drops in grade.

RaceName: Thirsk Sky Bet Summer Festival Day Friday 24th July Handicap
Spotlight: Sole win from 12 starts came in a Ripon novice in 2024 (1m, good); hasn't been the most consistent this season and he's been well beaten on his last two outings; down to a career-low mark but others are preferred.

RaceName: Thirsk Sky Bet Summer Festival Day Friday 24th July Handicap
Spotlight: Two wins from 29 starts, with the most recent success coming in April over this C&D; had cheekpieces back on (retained) when runner-up at Pontefract (1m, good) nine days ago and holds good claims off the same mark.

RaceName: Thirsk Sky Bet Summer Festival Day Friday 24th July Handicap
Spotlight: Off the mark when winning at Southwell (1m, AW) in May and followed up over this C&D next time (good); has posted two good placed efforts since, the most recent at Chester (7.5f, soft) last month off a 2lb lower mark than today's, and makes the shortlist.

RaceName: Thirsk Sky Bet Summer Festival Day Friday 24th July Handicap
Spotlight: Won at Ripon (1m, soft; Barley was second) last July off a mark 8lb higher than today's but he's struggled since; others have stronger claims.

RaceName: Thirsk Sky Bet Summer Festival Day Friday 24th July Handicap
Spotlight: Three-time winner, the most recent coming at Carlisle (1m, good to firm) in June 2024; placed in each of his last four starts, albeit he was below his best last time, and once again has an each-way chance.

RaceName: Irish EBF Auction Series Maiden (IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: Cheap purchase; third in Naas barrier trial in May; slowly into stride and never a factor at Fairyhouse (6f) last month; failed to improve in a 5f maiden at Bellewstown and now fitted with a hood.

RaceName: Irish EBF Auction Series Maiden (IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: Cost 42,000 euros as a yearling; tenth foal and half-brother to four winners; pedigree is perhaps more middle distance-oriented; one of two newcomers, both by Without Parole, for his trainer.

RaceName: Irish EBF Auction Series Maiden (IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: Cost 40,000 euros as a yearling;; third foal; half-brother by Cotai Glory to 6f winner Ceo Stealth Mode; dam unraced half-sister to winners Gangster Granny (Flat and hurdles) and Ballinea Star (6f 2yo Group 3), out of Group-placed 6f 2yo winner; potentially interesting.

RaceName: Irish EBF Auction Series Maiden (IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: Cost 30,000gns as a yearling; half-brother to seven winners over distances between 7f and 1m2f; dam French 1m 2yo winner

RaceName: Irish EBF Auction Series Maiden (IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: Ran green and did not get a clear passage at Roscommon; has a bit to find with Sea Of Rain (second) and Jezzie Bea (fourth); could feature with normal progress.

RaceName: Irish EBF Auction Series Maiden (IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: Cost 20,000 euros as a yearling; seventh foal; half-brother to winners Determined Effort (US 5f turf) and Karacrown (Mexican 2yo); dam French Listed-placed 1m 2yo/US 8.5f turf winner (RPR 98), half-sister to French 1m Listed winner Night Bokbel; jockey booking is noteworthy.

RaceName: Irish EBF Auction Series Maiden (IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: Not a bad run at a huge price on debut at the Curragh, will benefit from more experience.

RaceName: Irish EBF Auction Series Maiden (IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: Got upset in the stalls on debut at the Curragh; merit in her run in the circumstances, keeping on to finish sixth of 12; withdrawn after a stalls issue here last time; significant element of risk with that in mind.

RaceName: Irish EBF Auction Series Maiden (IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: Made some late headway over C&D on debut; potential for improvement but no reason why he should reverse form with runner-up Sea Of Rain.

RaceName: Irish EBF Auction Series Maiden (IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: Showed up well in a trial at Naas; failed to run to market expectations when 7-2 chance over C&D five weeks ago; rider reported she got upset in the stalls, due to the antics of the withdrawn Star Glory.

RaceName: Irish EBF Auction Series Maiden (IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: Left an impression that she still had plenty to learn when keeping on well at Roscommon on debut; fourth placing was promising in the circumstances; may get closer to runner-up Sea Of Rain; same remark could apply to fifth-placed Ballyskeagh.

RaceName: Irish EBF Auction Series Maiden (IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: Not disgraced at a big price on her introduction at the Curragh; might be one for handicaps in due course, if building on that.

RaceName: Irish EBF Auction Series Maiden (IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: Beaten favourite at Roscommon on debut, fourth behind a rival who had run twice; went within a whisker of opening her account over C&D on second start, beaten by a Jessica Harrington-trained favourite; beaten favourite for a second time at Roscommon; obvious prospects here, since she should again have the measure of Jezzie Bea and Ballyskeagh.

RaceName: DragonBet Welsh Racing Festival 9TH-11th Ooctober Handicap
Spotlight: Won a 6f handicap at Kempton in January and he has run several good races since, notably a narrow defeat back at Kempton two weeks ago; Billy Loughnane retains the ride; as this 5yo showed at Ascot in May, he's a strong contender back on fast turf.

RaceName: DragonBet Welsh Racing Festival 9TH-11th Ooctober Handicap
Spotlight: Multiple course winner, the latest last September (6f, soft) off a 1lb lower mark; she has run some solid races in defeat this year but consistency has been an issue and others appeal more in today's field.

RaceName: DragonBet Welsh Racing Festival 9TH-11th Ooctober Handicap
Spotlight: 5f win here (good ground) last August and he added two AW victories to his tally in December; his last two runs have been disappointing, the latest back on turf after an absence, but the return of Saffie Osborne to the saddle could perk him up (two wins and a second from the three times she has ridden him).

RaceName: DragonBet Welsh Racing Festival 9TH-11th Ooctober Handicap
Spotlight: Conditions to suit and this veteran is likely to find more races this year; the evidence of his two runs in 2026 suggests he may need some more help from the handicapper before he is of renewed interest though.

RaceName: DragonBet Welsh Racing Festival 9TH-11th Ooctober Handicap
Spotlight: The addition of cheekpieces saw her return to winning ways in a five-runner apprentice handicap at Salisbury (6f, good to firm) five days ago; 2lb well in but this is a deeper race.

RaceName: DragonBet Welsh Racing Festival 9TH-11th Ooctober Handicap
OwnerName: Mrs S M Maine & Roger Gould & Partner
Spotlight: In good form this year without managing to put his head in front; eligible for weaker races than this though and could prove vulnerable.

RaceName: DragonBet Welsh Racing Festival 9TH-11th Ooctober Handicap
OwnerName: Int Plywood (lmporters) Ltd And C Mason
Spotlight: C&D winner; shaped quite nicely at Windsor on his seasonal return but he failed to build on it at Ffos Las 17 days ago; others appeal more for the win.

RaceName: D. Ivo O'Sullivan Memorial Irish EBF Mares Hurdle
OwnerName: Ms Eileen O'Brien & John Anthony Harrington
Spotlight: Useful mare won a Grade 3 at Punchestown in February (2m4f, soft); outclassed in Grade 2 at Fairyhouse before finishing a 6.25l third behind Murcia over inadequate trip at Listowel (16.5f, good) on latest; this trip more her bag and may close the gap; any rain would be a plus.

RaceName: D. Ivo O'Sullivan Memorial Irish EBF Mares Hurdle
Spotlight: Grade 1 winner at Aintree (2m1f, gd-sft) in April 2025; regained the winning thread in mares' race at Listowel (16.5f, good) on latest (Grainne A Chroi 6.25l behind); the one to beat on ratings, but has never run beyond 2m1f so she has stamina to prove (mixed messages in pedigree).

RaceName: D. Ivo O'Sullivan Memorial Irish EBF Mares Hurdle
Spotlight: Solid placed graded form in France on testing ground; shed the maiden tag for this stable at Ludlow in February (2m5f, soft); not disgraced in sole start on good in the Grade 2 mares' novice at Cheltenham (2m1f); beaten 5l in Listed novice at Punchestown when last seen in April; Townend prefers Murcia.

RaceName: D. Ivo O'Sullivan Memorial Irish EBF Mares Hurdle
Spotlight: Bumper and maiden hurdle winner, both at around 2m on good to yielding and good to firm ground; soundly beaten behind a couple of these on return from a break at Listowel in May; more needed and stamina to prove over this far.

RaceName: D. Ivo O'Sullivan Memorial Irish EBF Mares Hurdle
Spotlight: 2-7 over hurdles and some smart form in the book; better known as a chaser these days, but has been out of sorts in that sphere on recent starts; third at Punchestown on latest, but was still beaten over 9l; needs to revive on first run over timber since March 2024.

RaceName: D. Ivo O'Sullivan Memorial Irish EBF Mares Hurdle
Spotlight: 0-7 over hurdles and rated just 88; out of her depth in this company.

RaceName: Hamilton Park Centenary Year Handicap
Spotlight: Finally got off the mark when a wide-margin winner over C&D three weeks ago; able to dictate the pace there so more is probably needed from a 6lb higher mark.

RaceName: Hamilton Park Centenary Year Handicap
Spotlight: Gained a breakthrough success at Newcastle (1m4f) in January; back from five months off in good nick, though seemed unsuited by the drop back in trip when fifth of eight at Nottingham (1m2f) 12 days ago; remains unexposed over this trip so very much one to consider.

RaceName: Hamilton Park Centenary Year Handicap
Spotlight: A dual 1m4f AW winner (also scored over hurdles) in 2025; running with credit without winning this season until only fourth of five at Beverley (1m4f) 13 days ago; he's the sort to bounce back in a change of headgear.

RaceName: Hamilton Park Centenary Year Handicap
Spotlight: A two-time winner (at up to 1m7f) last autumn; failed to build on earlier promise when only sixth of eight in 1m5f handicap at Ayr ten days ago; this course winner needs to get back on track.

RaceName: Hamilton Park Centenary Year Handicap
OwnerName: A Whillans, C Spark, J Wright
Spotlight: Winless since January 2024 but he arrives in good nick, third in 1m5f classified event at Ayr (disqualified for riding weighing in light) last month; not out of things back in handicap company.

RaceName: Prix Pelleas (Listed Race) (Colts & Geldings) (Turf)
Prix Pelleas (Listed Race) (Colts & Geldings) (Turf)
Prix Pelleas (Listed Race) (Colts & Geldings) (Turf)
OwnerName: Alain Jathiere, Ecurie David R Layani Famille Et Al

RaceName: Prix Pelleas (Listed Race) (Colts & Geldings) (Turf)
Prix Pelleas (Listed Race) (Colts & Geldings) (Turf)
Prix Pelleas (Listed Race) (Colts & Geldings) (Turf)
Bulmers Live At Leopardstown Apprentice Handicap
Spotlight: Enjoyed a good spell last summer, including a win and a short-head second in two outings over 1m here in June; twice made the frame over 1m here this year before winning over this trip at Down Royal in May; out of the money twice since then.

RaceName: Bulmers Live At Leopardstown Apprentice Handicap
Spotlight: Won 6f maiden for Ralph Beckett and later some decent efforts in defeat in France up to 1m2f; weak Irish form, in rear on both starts this season.

RaceName: Bulmers Live At Leopardstown Apprentice Handicap
Spotlight: Heavily-raced gelding; gained a tenth career win at Dundalk in April and seems to be holding his form well; 3l second at the Curragh and a fair fifth at Bellewstown 13 days ago; 0-11 record at this venue is a negative, placed three times.

RaceName: Bulmers Live At Leopardstown Apprentice Handicap
Spotlight: Ended last season with fine second placings at Down Royal and the Curragh; especially good effort behind the progressive Syzygy on the second occasion; no sign of similar form in three starts this term.

RaceName: Bulmers Live At Leopardstown Apprentice Handicap
Spotlight: Went up 12lb for a Fairyhouse success last month; beat subsequent winner Monastere then; however, unable to sustain her improvement when down the field at the same venue at the beginning of the month; held by Bear Right on that evidence.

RaceName: Bulmers Live At Leopardstown Apprentice Handicap
Spotlight: Placed over 7f on the second of three maiden starts at two; disappointing on only nursery attempt; after losing a prominent position, finished well for this rider to take third over 5f at Naas on her seasonal debut; every chance she can feature over this longer trip.

RaceName: Bulmers Live At Leopardstown Apprentice Handicap
Spotlight: Last win came in a 1m Killarney claimer in 2024; fifth in a claimer last time at Bellewstown, only 24 hours after finishing fourth in a handicap at the same venue; overall handicap form has been satisfactory; needs to raise his game

RaceName: Bulmers Live At Leopardstown Apprentice Handicap
Spotlight: Has made the frame on her last three starts; does not need much to improve from her recent close-up third at Fairyhouse; addition of cheekpieces may provide that little bit extra that she needs to get her head in front for the first time.

RaceName: Bulmers Live At Leopardstown Apprentice Handicap
Spotlight: Last season's 50-1 success in a 27-runner 6f contest at the Curragh now looks like a fluke; hard to fancy on this season's form; new headgear combination.

RaceName: Bulmers Live At Leopardstown Apprentice Handicap
Spotlight: Just one placed effort from eight starts last season; tailed off at the Curragh on his return and down the field at Roscommon two months ago; not a big price on the second occasion, so perhaps capable of better.

RaceName: Bulmers Live At Leopardstown Apprentice Handicap
Spotlight: Was in the form of his life around this time two seasons ago with back-to-back 6f wins for Michael O'Callaghan; signs of a revival when seventh of 21 at Naas last month; needs to provide further reassurance to become a betting proposition.

RaceName: Bulmers Live At Leopardstown Apprentice Handicap
Spotlight: Prolific AW winner (9-32); off-putting 1-31 record on turf; placed this season at Cork and the Curragh; below best on three most recent starts.

RaceName: Bulmers Live At Leopardstown Apprentice Handicap
Spotlight: Five-time turf winner; out of sorts on AW last winter, unseated rider at start on reappearance at Bellewstown 13 days ago; jockey booking is the main positive.

RaceName: Bulmers Live At Leopardstown Apprentice Handicap
Spotlight: AW winner over 1m; that had seemed like a flash in the pan until a revival in form when third behind the well-fancied Glen Breeze at Limerick last Saturday; 2lb wrong here but not without a chance of reaching the money.

RaceName: Chepstow Family Fun Day 31st August Handicap
Spotlight: Tailed off at Ffos Las (1m4f, good to soft) on her stable/handicap debut last month; left that well behind when springing a 33-1 surprise here (1m4f, good to firm) last week; 4lb wrong under a penalty and not obviously crying out for today's drop in distance but as a last-time winner with few miles on the clock she can't be discounted.

RaceName: Chepstow Family Fun Day 31st August Handicap
Spotlight: Two 1m wins on good ground last summer, including at this track; well short of that level in two runs this year and stepping up in trip with cheekpieces added isn't enough to tempt.

RaceName: Chepstow Family Fun Day 31st August Handicap
Spotlight: 0-14 but six times a runner-up and he also ran well in a similar race over C&D last year; his form dipped last time but that was his first run on soft ground; likely to go well.

RaceName: Chepstow Family Fun Day 31st August Handicap
Spotlight: Well beaten in three novice/maiden runs (7f/1m); much more encouragement when keeping on for second on last month's handicap debut (1m4f, AW), still looking green in the process; makes his turf debut in a weak race and with Billy Loughane back in the saddle; big chance.

RaceName: Adare Manor Opportunity Handicap Hurdle (Div I)
Spotlight: Has improved for better ground; edging up the weights after placed efforts at Punchestown (3m, gd-yld), Listowel (20.5f, gd-yld) and on latest at Limerick (22.5f, good); nudged up another 2lb, but should be on the premises again.

RaceName: Adare Manor Opportunity Handicap Hurdle (Div I)
Spotlight: 0-7 over hurdles but has run well on two of last three starts; beaten 15l when runner up in maiden hurdle at Wexford in May; hung badly left when pulled up on handicap debut at Sligo; solid third off this mark at Bellewstown latest; chance if building on that.

RaceName: Adare Manor Opportunity Handicap Hurdle (Div I)
Spotlight: 0-5 over hurdles but did show some promise in two of his maidens; pulled up on handicap bow at Ballinrobe (2m, good) on latest, however, and needs to leave that behind upped in trip.

RaceName: Adare Manor Opportunity Handicap Hurdle (Div I)
Spotlight: Arguably best effort yet on handicap debut at Kilbeggan (15.5f, gd-yld) when keeping on from the back for a 9l fifth of 13; bred to appreciate this longer trip; could go well.

RaceName: Adare Manor Opportunity Handicap Hurdle (Div I)
Spotlight: Flat winner on fast ground for David O'Meara; huge odds and well held in three maiden hurdles for this yard at 2m-2m1f; potential improver stepped up in trip for handicap debut.

RaceName: Adare Manor Opportunity Handicap Hurdle (Div I)
Spotlight: Struggled in all three maiden hurdles on soft and soft to heavy ground; given a break since soundly beaten at Clonmel and tongue tie goes on for handicap debut; best watched unless the market speaks.

RaceName: Adare Manor Opportunity Handicap Hurdle (Div I)
Spotlight: Conditions are fine for this veteran; has gone well here before and he's well handicapped but hasn't won since October 2024 and his recent efforts have been well below par; revival needed.

RaceName: Adare Manor Opportunity Handicap Hurdle (Div I)
Spotlight: Best effort yet on return from 131 days off at Kilbeggan (17.5f, gd-yld) when a 7.75l fourth of 13; should strip fitter now, eased 1lb and pedigree suggests that this trip might suit; not discounted.

RaceName: Adare Manor Opportunity Handicap Hurdle (Div I)
Spotlight: Point winner off the mark under rules at Fairyhouse in October off 6lb lower (2m, yielding); held off revised mark twice since, though may have needed latest; step up in trip needs to unlock further improvement.

RaceName: Adare Manor Opportunity Handicap Hurdle (Div I)
Spotlight: Didn't show a whole lot in maidens and similar story in first two handicaps; assessor relenting, but others have more appealing profiles.

RaceName: Adare Manor Opportunity Handicap Hurdle (Div I)
Spotlight: Maiden whose three best efforts have come on testing ground at Limerick; has a race in him, but has to prove he acts on this quicker ground.

RaceName: Adare Manor Opportunity Handicap Hurdle (Div I)
Spotlight: Long-standing maiden ran okay at Listowel last September, but was still beaten 11.5l into third; mid-field over 2m7f here when last seen in October; may need this on return.

RaceName: Adare Manor Opportunity Handicap Hurdle (Div I)
Spotlight: Maiden pointer; 0-15 over hurdles and has yet to display winning potential; soundly beaten again at Kilbeggan on return from break latest; others preferred.

RaceName: Adare Manor Opportunity Handicap Hurdle (Div I)
Spotlight: Won off 8lb higher at Galway in September 2024 but has shown virtually nothing since; tailed off at Sligo on latest and impossible to fancy on recent form.

RaceName: Adare Manor Opportunity Handicap Hurdle (Div I)
Spotlight: 0-7 and yet to trouble the judge; a bit better at Kilbeggan on latest (24.5f, good), but much more needed dropped back in trip.

RaceName: Betfred "The Classic Bookmaker" EBF Maiden Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Third foal; Kingman half-brother to winners San Juanito (8.6f/9.4f; RPR 89) and Proposal (7f; 77); dam 7f 2yo/1m2f Group 3 winner (116) out of 7f-1m4f winner; newcomer to note from strong yard.

RaceName: Betfred "The Classic Bookmaker" EBF Maiden Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: 48,000gns yearling by Kodi Bear; first foal; dam 5f winner (RPR 68) out of 6f 2yo Group 3 winning half-sister to 7f Group 1 winner Toylsome; others are a bit more striking on paper.

RaceName: Betfred "The Classic Bookmaker" EBF Maiden Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Seventh foal; Palace Pier half-brother to four winners including Great David (Listed-placed 1m 2yo/1m2f; RPR 105), Kinderfrau (1m5f/2m; 87) and Leadership (UAE 7f/1m; 84); dam Group-placed 1m-10.4f winner (107) out of 12.5f Group 2 winner; trainer's 2yos have 25% strike-rate (6-24) this term.

RaceName: Betfred "The Classic Bookmaker" EBF Maiden Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: 300,000gns yearling by Lope De Vega; half-brother to four winners, mostly useful or smart, notably Berkshire Rocco (7f-2m including 2yo/Listed; RPR 117); 5-1 for novice at Sandown (7f, good) 13 days ago but slowly into stride and, having raced in mid-division, readily outpaced from 3f out; that 15l defeat was a downbeat start and he now has his tongue tied.

RaceName: Betfred "The Classic Bookmaker" EBF Maiden Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: 190,000gns yearling by Zarak; first foal of Group-placed 5f 2yo winner (RPR 99), herself out of 1m Group 1 winner; 9-2 from 7-1 for novice at Wolverhampton (7f, AW) seven weeks ago, led early and went okay turning for home, but she was last of six from over 1f out; needs to leave that way behind.

RaceName: CopyBet Bog Today For Tomorrow's Racing Handicap Chase (Arc Summer Chase Series Qualifier)
Spotlight: Multiple hurdle winner who is rated 12lb lower over fences and he rediscovered some form when beaten 2l at Cartmel three weeks ago (2m5f, good); should be bang there if repeating that effort.

RaceName: CopyBet Bog Today For Tomorrow's Racing Handicap Chase (Arc Summer Chase Series Qualifier)
Spotlight: Has left his hurdle form behind over fences and he beat the odds-on winner with the pair clear at Stratford last weekend (2m3f, good to firm); carries a penalty for that in what looks a winnable contest.

RaceName: CopyBet Bog Today For Tomorrow's Racing Handicap Chase (Arc Summer Chase Series Qualifier)
Spotlight: Won twice over fences within a week last December but has essentially struggled off his new mark and still 6lb higher than for those two wins; still commands some respect in this weak a race.

RaceName: CopyBet Bog Today For Tomorrow's Racing Handicap Chase (Arc Summer Chase Series Qualifier)
Spotlight: Record of 1-14 in Irish points; 0-9 over hurdles (was placed); made a quiet stable debut in that discipline and he was pulled up in a hunter chase on one previous run over fences.

RaceName: CopyBet Bog Today For Tomorrow's Racing Handicap Chase (Arc Summer Chase Series Qualifier)
Spotlight: Struggles for consistency and has gone backwards since finishing third at Hereford in March (3m1f, good); first-time blinkers did nothing for him last time and they're retained.

RaceName: CopyBet Bog Today For Tomorrow's Racing Handicap Chase (Arc Summer Chase Series Qualifier)
Spotlight: Eight defeats under rules include seven as a chaser; forgettable form in Irish points this year.

RaceName: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Fillies Maiden (IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: Good American pedigree and cost $150,000 as a yearling; 25s, prominent until weakening quickly 1f out in hot 6f maiden at the Curragh on Guineas weekend.

RaceName: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Fillies Maiden (IRE Incentive Race)
OwnerName: Mrs E Irwin & Mrs John Magnier
Spotlight: St Mark's Basilica half-sister to eight winners including Australian Group 2 winner Adjusted; dam won over 1m1f in France; first home in a barrier trial before last week's debut here over 7f; 7-2, finished off well when second to a promising type; entered in the Group 1 Moyglare; slightly unusual that she's out again so quickly.

RaceName: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Fillies Maiden (IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: By Prix de l'Abbaye winner Wooded and cost 41,000 euros as a yearling; half-sister to French 6f-7.5f winner Viva La Skids (inc 2yo; RPR 103); dam unplaced 6f-1m2f in France, out of half-sister to US 1m2f Grade 1 winner Desert Blanc; missed Naas Listed race (coughing) in which Sarah's Choice was second, and was apparent stable second-string for that.

RaceName: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Fillies Maiden (IRE Incentive Race)
OwnerName: Mrs John Magnier & Michael Tabor & Derrick Smith
Spotlight: By multiple champion US sire Into Mischief; half-sister to five winners, notably Derby winner City Of Troy, out of a Group 1 winner; 16-1, slowly away and never got in to it at the Curragh in 7f maiden where stablemates were first and second; entered in the Moyglare, though, and no surprise to see a much better showing this time.

RaceName: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Fillies Maiden (IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: Cost 100,000 euros at the breeze-ups and is speedily bred; 10-1, caught late over 1m at Naas in Listed race won by rival now rated 89, so it's not form to get carried away with and had a relatively hard race into the bargain; backs up fairly quickly as well, but obviously has a good level of ability.

RaceName: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Fillies Maiden (IRE Incentive Race)
OwnerName: Derrick Smith & Mrs John Magnier & Michael Tabor
Spotlight: By Wootton Bassett; first living foal out of Love, who won on her third start at two before going on to win the Moyglare, and completed the 1,000 Guineas/Oaks double the following year; holds a Moyglare entry.

RaceName: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Fillies Maiden (IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: By State Of Rest, who was trained by Joseph O'Brien to win Group 1 races in four different countries, including the Cox Plate; cost 42,000 euros as a yearling; half-sister to 6f AW 2yo winner Karaoke; dam US 6.5f dirt winner, half-sister to BC Juvenile Turf winner Hootenanny.

RaceName: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Fillies Maiden (IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: Daughter of Australia was bought back for 18,000 euro at the yearling sales; closely related to 6f/7f winner Orchidaceae (inc 2yo; RPR 98), half-sister to 1m AW winner Another Day Done; dam 7f 2yo winner, half-sister to 7f 2yo Listed winner Mountain Bear; stable second-string and no fancy entries.

RaceName: CRE Run Farm Handicap Stakes 0-60
Spotlight: Has looked a work in progress on both starts to date; this looks his easiest task to date as cheekpieces go on for the first time and improvement is likely at some stage.  (R2 W0 P0) (OR 60)

RaceName: CRE Run Farm Handicap Stakes 0-60
Spotlight: Having a third quick run with cheekpieces replacing a visor; should find this company a bit easier than her first two runs and with a positive jockey booking is likely to go close. (R2 W0 P2) (OR 59)

RaceName: CRE Run Farm Handicap Stakes 0-60
Spotlight: No match for runaway winner at Lingfield last week but finished a long way clear of the rest.; conditions here are fine for her and she's capable of winning of this mark; solid claims. (R21 W2 P12) (OR 55)

RaceName: CRE Run Farm Handicap Stakes 0-60
Spotlight: Won twice last season and has now returned to her last winning mark; back down to a more suitable grade, at a track she likes better can be expected this time. (R17 W3 P7) (OR 54)

RaceName: CRE Run Farm Handicap Stakes 0-60
Spotlight: Looked in need of the experience on both starts to date; makes her seasonal and handicap debut off a workable mark and is open to lots of improvement for the Champion Trainer; the market will reveal more. (R2 W0 P0) (OR 52)

RaceName: CRE Run Farm Handicap Stakes 0-60
Spotlight: Unplaced twice last season and return here off a mark where improvement is likely; from a late maturing family and should do better this season, market moves should be noted. (R2 W0 P0) (OR 52)

RaceName: CRE Run Farm Handicap Stakes 0-60
Spotlight: Hasn't really kicked on after her winning here final start as a juvenile; her latest run in Listed company was a step back in the right direction and has to be respected if in the same form. (R11 W1 P7) (OR 52)

RaceName: CRE Run Farm Handicap Stakes 0-60
Spotlight: Course winner who can often be slowly away; when in the mood she usually finishes strongly and she stays well; didn't show much on her seasonal debut but might improve for the run; small each way claims in a very open heat. (R23 W3 P5) (OR 37)

RaceName: Adare Manor Opportunity Handicap Hurdle (Div II)
Spotlight: Four wins over hurdles, the most recent coming in this race last year off 2lb lower on yielding to soft, his second win over C&D; out of depth on the Flat last time, but this is more suitable and acts on good ground; player.

RaceName: Adare Manor Opportunity Handicap Hurdle (Div II)
Spotlight: Solid placed maiden form on good ground last season; struggled in testing conditions the last twice, including on handicap debut at Cork in December; returns from a break now with ground to suit, so check the market.

RaceName: Adare Manor Opportunity Handicap Hurdle (Div II)
OwnerName: Eamon Nolan & Mrs Catherine Anne Nolan
Spotlight: Glimmers of promise in maidens before fine 1l second on handicap debut at Limerick in March (2m, soft); didn't quite see out 2m4f at Wexford on latest (gd-yld); stamina a query again here.

RaceName: Adare Manor Opportunity Handicap Hurdle (Div II)
Spotlight: Veteran's five career wins include a handicap chase here in August 2024 (2m5f, yielding); that's the last time he won and pulled up on return from long layoff at Listowel 47 days ago.

RaceName: Adare Manor Opportunity Handicap Hurdle (Div II)
Spotlight: Flat winner improved on maiden hurdle runs when a 6.5l fifth of 15 on handicap debut at Sligo four days ago, racing a shade keenly and weakening late; place shout off the same mark if settling better.

RaceName: Adare Manor Opportunity Handicap Hurdle (Div II)
Spotlight: 0-9 over hurdles; best efforts have come on soft and soft to heavy ground; beaten 15l on soft at Limerick on latest and only 1lb lower here; more needed.

RaceName: Adare Manor Opportunity Handicap Hurdle (Div II)
Spotlight: Two wins last season included one here over 2m7f (yielding) off 4lb lower; has also won a chase here and is 2-3 at this venue; should be sharper for latest run after break over an inadequate trip, but probably not much wriggle room off this mark.

RaceName: Adare Manor Opportunity Handicap Hurdle (Div II)
Spotlight: Left modest maiden form behind to run a fine race when 1.75l second on handicap debut at Clonmel (19.5f, gd-yld); stayed on well from the back that day so whether this sharper track will suit is up for debate, but unexposed and enters calculations.

RaceName: Adare Manor Opportunity Handicap Hurdle (Div II)
Spotlight: AW winner on the Flat; 0-5 over hurdles and pulled up in first two handicaps; can only be watched for now.

RaceName: Adare Manor Opportunity Handicap Hurdle (Div II)
Spotlight: 1-24 over hurdles, the win coming over 3m on heavy ground at Clonmel in 2023; is now 12lb lower, but has disappointed the last twice after showing a bit more at Clonmel in May.

RaceName: Adare Manor Opportunity Handicap Hurdle (Div II)
Spotlight: Showed little in maidens and well beaten on handicap debut here over 2m1f last October (2m1f, yielding); brought down two out when near the rear at Downpatrick on return from break; a bit to prove.

RaceName: Adare Manor Opportunity Handicap Hurdle (Div II)
Spotlight: 16-race maiden over hurdles; her Sligo third from last July off 3lb higher would give her a squeak, but well below that level in two runs since returning from a break, the latest over fences; more needed.

RaceName: Adare Manor Opportunity Handicap Hurdle (Div II)
Spotlight: Off the mark off 6lb lower in Tramore handicap hurdle in April (20.5f, yielding); came up short off revised rating at Clonmel; was feeling the pinch when unseating two out at Tramore latest; place shout.

RaceName: Adare Manor Opportunity Handicap Hurdle (Div II)
Spotlight: Consistent and holding form well enough in handicaps; beaten around 6l off this mark the last twice, at Down Royal (2m4f, good) and Wexford (2m1f, gd-yld) when wearing first-time cheekpieces (retained); step back up to this trip should suit; in the mix again.

RaceName: Adare Manor Opportunity Handicap Hurdle (Div II)
Spotlight: Maiden seemed to appreciate the step up to 22.5f (good) at Clonmel last time when keeping on for a 9.25l third; eased 1lb, but not sure whether the drop back in trip here will suit.

RaceName: Steve Donoghue Handicap
Spotlight: The only impact he made in three turf races (last April-July) came when third on good ground at Chester, which is up with his best AW form; pretty reliable with his minor AW honours this year, so needs considering.

RaceName: Steve Donoghue Handicap
OwnerName: J Machale, S Tucker & S Jewell
Spotlight: 5-21 on AW compared to 0-10 on turf and he's been well beaten in his two races here; turf mark is 12lb below AW figure and he should have come on since his recent, creditable reappearance, but others are more persuasive.

RaceName: Steve Donoghue Handicap
Spotlight: Three wins this year, the last two at Bath (1m/1m2f) in April and she's continued to look in good form, beaten a head at Salisbury (1m) on latest start; firmly in the mix if she adapts to this track.

RaceName: Steve Donoghue Handicap
Spotlight: Front-runner; latest win was under Mason Paetel here (1m2f) early last July for Ed Dunlop; 4lb lower today but that's after he went off the boil and there's been only a partial revival for new yard this year, finishing sixth of nine here last time in the race he won last year; the last time he raced over a trip this short was May 2025.

RaceName: Steve Donoghue Handicap
OwnerName: M H Dixon & J L Rowsell
Spotlight: 3yo who's had respectable efforts in her first four handicaps, before a lesser show from wide draw at Kempton latest; place chance if back to her best.

RaceName: Steve Donoghue Handicap
Spotlight: 14-race maiden; latest of his three seconds was in April 2025 and he's not made a serious impact since; off since January and hard to fancy.

RaceName: Steve Donoghue Handicap
Spotlight: Marked improvement is needed but he's had just four races; fifth of ten on seasonal/handicap debut at Newmarket (1m, good) four weeks ago failed to match his market position but he staged something of a rally, after hanging left over 2f out, and might build on that.

RaceName: Steve Donoghue Handicap
Spotlight: 15-race maiden who's in his third season; runner-up over 1m2f in one of his two visits here last summer and two minor honours (7.6f/1m) from three starts this term; cheekpieces (tried once as 3yo, well held) return but he does not look the percentage call for win purposes.

RaceName: Steve Donoghue Handicap
Spotlight: Eight-race maiden and his best show in five handicaps was on first occasion (1m, AW); 8lb lower today but lacks any pressing claims.

RaceName: CopyBet Pay First Past The Post Handicap Chase (Arc Summer Chase Series Qualifier)
Spotlight: Admirable veteran who nearly recorded his ninth hurdle win when a close second here (2m7f, good) in May; 0-8 over fences but he's 12lb lower back in this sphere and he does have some placed form in chase handicaps; dangerous with blinkers reapplied.

RaceName: CopyBet Pay First Past The Post Handicap Chase (Arc Summer Chase Series Qualifier)
Spotlight: Maiden hurdler but he's been a big improver since switched to fences, winning over 3m at Warwick last month before following up over about 2m4f here (both on good) two weeks ago; up another 7lb but he won going away last time and is a major player again in his bid to make it 3-4 as a chaser.

RaceName: CopyBet Pay First Past The Post Handicap Chase (Arc Summer Chase Series Qualifier)
Spotlight: No win in five starts over hurdles but he made it 2-4 over fences when beating eight rivals at Market Rasen (3m, good) 11 days ago; has a penalty but that was a cosy success and he's open to more progress at staying trips; respected.

RaceName: CopyBet Pay First Past The Post Handicap Chase (Arc Summer Chase Series Qualifier)
Spotlight: Got off the mark when winning a Huntingdon handicap (2m4f, soft) in February and he's finished runner-up in four over his five starts since; his latest second was behind Knightsbridge at Warwick but he has big swing in the weights with that rival and should be in the thick of things again.

RaceName: CopyBet Pay First Past The Post Handicap Chase (Arc Summer Chase Series Qualifier)
OwnerName: K Johns & Joe Tickle Racing Club
Spotlight: Five-time chase winner, most recently at Windsor (3m, good to soft) in the autumn; back on a good mark now but he's not easy to predict and has been pulled up on two of his last three outings (bled from the nose last time); others preferred.

RaceName: CopyBet Pay First Past The Post Handicap Chase (Arc Summer Chase Series Qualifier)
Spotlight: Well treated on his old form but his last win was in December 2024 and he's finished down the field in his three completed starts this year; cheekpieces now return but he needs to raise his game.

RaceName: CopyBet Pay First Past The Post Handicap Chase (Arc Summer Chase Series Qualifier)
Spotlight: Irish point winner who has yet to show any worthwhile form under rules and was tailed off on chase debut over C&D (good) last month; still lightly raced but cheekpieces hasn't worked in his last three runs and he needs a visor to make a big difference.

RaceName: CopyBet Pay First Past The Post Handicap Chase (Arc Summer Chase Series Qualifier)
Spotlight: Just one win from 25 starts over hurdles and she's generally struggled in that sphere in last seven starts; pulled up in only previous chase run in February 2025 and has lots to prove back in this sphere; 5lb out of the weights.

RaceName: Dublin To Bahrain Handicap
Spotlight: Unlucky a few times on turf last year, but won three times at Dundalk, notably when beating subsequent Group 2 winner Jancis in a Listed contest over this trip; favourite/joint favourite all three outings in premier handicaps this term, twice running well at the Curragh over 6f/7f; mark remains 7lb lower than AW rating; doesn't usually lead, but can race handily.

RaceName: Dublin To Bahrain Handicap
Spotlight: Front-running second over this trip at Gowran leaves him with a little bit to find with How'sthebai on a line through the winner; went one better at the Curragh, and third's subsequent run suggests this opening mark is about right; scratched at Killarney on Monday due to ground described as good.

RaceName: Dublin To Bahrain Handicap
OwnerName: Thomas J O'Connor & Ms Hazel McGuinness
Spotlight: Nine wins in all, but only one on turf from 32 starts; led when third over 7f at Fairyhouse last week, and that's probably his optimum trip on turf, rather than the stiff mile here; unplaced all four runs here, too.

RaceName: Dublin To Bahrain Handicap
Spotlight: Likeable 3yo; won AW maiden last year; good fourth in hot Naas handicap over 1m before making most to beat in-form rival over 1m1f on probably unsuitably soft ground here; had that rival a little further back when fourth in 1m1f premier handicap at the Curragh, where missing a beat at the start proved costly in a race where the leaders didn't come back; takes on older horses for the first time.

RaceName: Dublin To Bahrain Handicap
Spotlight: Three wins on AW, but 0-21 on turf; did well at Dundalk over the winter and carried that form through to soft-ground contests in the spring; second over 7f at Naas and fourth over 1m2f here in April (strong form); not so good since; likes to come from off the pace, and there wasn't enough on when he was fourth (only 7-2) at Killarney on Monday.

RaceName: Dublin To Bahrain Handicap
Spotlight: Will like the ground; won over 1m1f here last year and has dropped back to just 1lb higher mark; ground too soft first two starts this year; latest 6l Curragh defeat can be marked up somewhat (upset in stalls and met some interference) and generally runs this track well.

RaceName: Noel C. Duggan Structural Steel 110 Series Final Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Dual hurdle winner in France; ran respectably on Irish debut at Limerick after a long absence in a race where the winner was on a faster wide strip of ground; should have improved from that and has a shout.

RaceName: Noel C. Duggan Structural Steel 110 Series Final Handicap Hurdle
OwnerName: Exors of the Late E Blaney
Spotlight: Dual Flat winner is only 1-18 over hurdles, but has put up solid runs the last twice at Downpatrick; the blinkers worn last time are retained and can be involved, but needs more to win.

RaceName: Noel C. Duggan Structural Steel 110 Series Final Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Flat winner has shown much-improved form over hurdles this season, winning at Clonmel and Limerick the last twice over approximately this trip; another 7lb rise demands more, but he has to be on the shortlist.

RaceName: Noel C. Duggan Structural Steel 110 Series Final Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Hurdle and chase winner when trained in the UK; hit and miss, kept to hurdles, since switched to this yard; well beaten when last seen and others appeal more today.

RaceName: Noel C. Duggan Structural Steel 110 Series Final Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Won a novice chase at Cork last October, but is a maiden over hurdles; well beaten in this sphere at Ballinrobe last time over 2m, but this trip could suit; is capable of better, so not ruled out.

RaceName: Noel C. Duggan Structural Steel 110 Series Final Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Has been in fine form since the spring, winning a 2m6f Down Royal novice handicap before runner-up in Cork and Listowel handicaps over 3m; should be fine dropped in trip and blinkers replace cheekpieces; up 2lb; player.

RaceName: Noel C. Duggan Structural Steel 110 Series Final Handicap Hurdle
