Spotlight: Some decent efforts in defeat in maidens and handicaps in varying ground conditions; beaten favourite in a Clonmel maiden last time; steps up in trip today and a tongue-tie added; needs more but not ruled out.

RaceName: Noel C. Duggan Structural Steel 110 Series Final Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Chase winner has won twice over hurdles with last score coming at Clonmel in June 2025; some decent runs this term, but was beaten a long way at Sligo four days ago.

RaceName: Noel C. Duggan Structural Steel 110 Series Final Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Got off the mark in a Kilbeggan handicap hurdle in May and a solid run in a Cartmel chase since; 5lb higher than for his win and is up in grade too, but can run well.

RaceName: Noel C. Duggan Structural Steel 110 Series Final Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Maiden was beaten 5l by today's rival Global Skies at Clonmel last time, his best handicap run; raised 2lb for that and, if he has progressed since, can run respectably again.

RaceName: Chantilly Handicap
Spotlight: Ex-French 4yo who ran his best race for this yard when beaten half a length from the back at Doncaster (1m4f, good to soft) last time; back up 2lb; unraced on firmer than good; major player if it does not prove a problem, and his trainer won this race in 2023 and 2025.

RaceName: Chantilly Handicap
Spotlight: Held up; ran creditably sole start here (2024); latest of his two wins came when dropped into Class 6 at Bath (1m2f, firm) in May, before a Class 4 at Yarmouth saw a lesser effort; should be competitive in this race.

RaceName: Chantilly Handicap
OwnerName: Mrs F Cotton, Mr & Mrs P Conway
Spotlight: Best efforts from his five handicaps were on last two starts when third at Goodwood (1m1f, good to soft) and beaten a head at Newbury (1m2f, good to firm); back up 2lb but his first win should not be far away.

RaceName: Chantilly Handicap
Spotlight: Spent last year in France; fine record here, including two wins in summer 2024 and a third off this mark in a blanket finish two weeks ago; the latter followed a Windsor win (1m2f, win); looks set for another big run.

RaceName: Chantilly Handicap
Spotlight: Reliable 3yo whose two wins (from 13 races) were in very small fields, latterly when making all at Newmarket (1m2f, good to firm) three weeks ago; that was a runaway win and the first time he's made the running, but he's 5lb higher today in what looks a more competitive race.

RaceName: CopyBet Support Safer Gambling Maiden Hurdle (Arc Summer Novices' Brush Hurdle Qualifier) (GBB)
Spotlight: Below best last time but the start before he had run well (looked held) before falling late on at Warwick (2m3f, good); every chance on that form.

RaceName: CopyBet Support Safer Gambling Maiden Hurdle (Arc Summer Novices' Brush Hurdle Qualifier) (GBB)
Spotlight: Finished last in his only bumper and beaten between 15l and 36l in three maiden hurdles (2m2f-2m4f); hard to make a compelling case for him but he has shown something and this is weak.

RaceName: CopyBet Support Safer Gambling Maiden Hurdle (Arc Summer Novices' Brush Hurdle Qualifier) (GBB)
Spotlight: Third in a Warwick bumper; had a wind operation prior to finishing a 10l third over 2m4f at Southwell last month; this longer trip looks the way to go and should run well.

RaceName: CopyBet Support Safer Gambling Maiden Hurdle (Arc Summer Novices' Brush Hurdle Qualifier) (GBB)
Spotlight: Comfortable winner of a weak bumper at Hexham but was a remote sixth back there when long odds-on for her hurdle debut (2m4f); that was probably too bad to be true.

RaceName: CopyBet Support Safer Gambling Maiden Hurdle (Arc Summer Novices' Brush Hurdle Qualifier) (GBB)
HorseName: Snow Is In The Sky IRE
Spotlight: Beat two finishers in an Irish point; completely tailed off in two maiden hurdles.

RaceName: CopyBet Support Safer Gambling Maiden Hurdle (Arc Summer Novices' Brush Hurdle Qualifier) (GBB)
Spotlight: Dual point winner but has looked very limited under rules; 300-1 when pulled up here 15 days ago; the tongue-tie is new.

RaceName: CopyBet Support Safer Gambling Maiden Hurdle (Arc Summer Novices' Brush Hurdle Qualifier) (GBB)
Spotlight: Ex-Irish mare who was tailed off in an Irish bumper and gone 0-10 in points there; new headgear combination on yard debut.

RaceName: BOYLE Sports Meld Stakes (Group 3)
Spotlight: Second in the Irish Derby and Grand Prix de Paris for Aidan O'Brien in 2023; mostly below that level in Australia, apart from a nose second in 1m2f Group 3 at Randwick last September; marked his return to Ireland with a pleasing staying-on third to Minnie Hauk in 1m2f Curragh Group 2 (good) in May; failed to feature in Royal Ascot's Wolferton Stakes.

RaceName: BOYLE Sports Meld Stakes (Group 3)
Spotlight: Group 3 winner over 7f at Deauville as a 2yo; lost way last season but back to form this term; runner-up to City Of Memphis in conditions race at Gowran (7f, soft) and won conditions race at Fairyhouse (7f, good); fair third of six in a 1m Listed race at the Curragh on latest.

RaceName: BOYLE Sports Meld Stakes (Group 3)
Spotlight: Three starts at Royal Ascot have yielded a win in the 2023 Chesham (7f), an excellent second of 23 in the Kensington Palace (1m) last year, and a creditable fifth in last month's Duke of Cambridge (1m); produced her best display on home soil when beating the odds-on Purview in this race 12 months ago; began this season with a solid third in a 7f Group 3 at this track.

RaceName: BOYLE Sports Meld Stakes (Group 3)
Spotlight: Progressive handicapper on heavy ground late last season; began this season with an excellent third in the 25-runner Irish Lincoln; placed in a 1m handicap at this venue in April; career-best on latest when chasing home stablemate and subsequent Group 3 winner Duckadilly in a 1m Listed race at Navan; 50-1 then, and is the outsider on figures here.

RaceName: BOYLE Sports Meld Stakes (Group 3)
Spotlight: Smart Curragh form at two; beat top-class filly Kensington Lane on debut and then finished second in Group 2 races won by Constitution River and Hawk Mountain; 2026 form includes links to top-class performers Thesecretadversary and Causeway; a promoted second behind Isaac Newton in a 1m1f Listed event at Navan; reversed form with that rival on latest.

RaceName: BOYLE Sports Meld Stakes (Group 3)
Spotlight: Goodwood maiden winner on debut last August (7f, good to soft); creditable run in Group 1 at Saint-Cloud (1m2f, very soft) in October; moderate efforts first two runs this term; back on song when winning a 1m1f Listed race at Navan (Geryon promoted to second); lost a lot of ground at the start when failing to confirm form with Geryon at the Curragh last time.

RaceName: Kerry Drains Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Sligo maiden winner last year hasn't matched that form since; was poor at Punchestown, but was better at Downpatrick since; needs a return to his best.

RaceName: Kerry Drains Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Wexford maiden hurdle winner for Joseph O'Brien last year; however, has been below that form in four runs for this yard; blinkers replace previously-worn cheekpieces.

RaceName: Kerry Drains Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Maiden has been placed in bumpers and hurdles, but has been poor the last thrice in handicaps and has to do much better.

RaceName: Kerry Drains Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Maiden hurdle winner in 2023 and also two Flat wins for Tony Martin; won a Kilbeggan handicap on stable debut returning from a 214-day break; needs more up 7lb but a player.

RaceName: Kerry Drains Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Useful Flat sort on the continent got off the mark over hurdles last time at Downpatrick; got an 11lb hike for that, but goes well fresh and is still rated 90 on the Flat, so has to be respected.

RaceName: Kerry Drains Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Won back-to-back handicaps last year for Tony Mullins, but lost his form after; hit and miss chasing lately for this yard, but would have a chance if back to his best.

RaceName: Kerry Drains Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Dual bumper winner in France; won a Thurles handicap hurdle last October, but was poor at Cheltenham next time; market can guide on return with yard in form.

RaceName: Kerry Drains Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Promise in first two bumpers; soundly beaten in maiden hurdles, but can do better in this sphere; one of three for a yard that does well here, so watch the market.

RaceName: Kerry Drains Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Promise in a bumper, but well beaten on first two hurdle starts; much-improved when fourth in a Roscommon maiden last time; has a shout if building on that.

RaceName: Kerry Drains Handicap Hurdle
OwnerName: Exors of the Late M Mortell
Spotlight: Won at Listowel last September, but has been well below that level twice this term; needs a return to best; second of three runners for yard.

RaceName: Kerry Drains Handicap Hurdle
OwnerName: John Joseph Flynn & Conor Lannen & Mrs Miriam Queally
Spotlight: Beaten a long way in a bumper and three hurdles, so has to improve loads on handicap bow off what looks a stiff mark.

RaceName: Kerry Drains Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Flat winner won a 2m1f Wexford handicap and a solid run off this mark there since; needs more up in grade, but shouldn't be far away.

RaceName: Kerry Drains Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Record of 1-18 over hurdles; third in a Clonmel claimer in May, but was beaten a long way on debut for this yard at Downpatrcik since; has to rebound.

RaceName: Kerry Drains Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Winless, but some good runs, since taking a Listowel maiden hurdle in 2024; well beaten chasing last twice, but can't be ruled out back in this discipline with an in-form rider aboard; third runner for yard; watch market.

RaceName: Kerry Drains Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Hasn't won since May 2023, but some solid runs last year; however, is best watched on return unless strong in the market.

RaceName: Betfred Ladies' Derby Handicap (For Female Amateur Jockeys) (GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: All wins have been at 1m2f and he ran well over the extended 1m here on reappearance, before a lesser show at Sandown; his record at this track is very solid, but those on good to firm and at 1m4f less so.

RaceName: Betfred Ladies' Derby Handicap (For Female Amateur Jockeys) (GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: Six-race maiden whose easily best display in three handicaps came when fifth of ten at Chester (1m2f) in June; the lesser shows on either side were over about 1m3f; blinkers (worn on last two starts) are removed; 7lb lower than on handicap/seasonal debut but something to prove.

RaceName: Betfred Ladies' Derby Handicap (For Female Amateur Jockeys) (GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: 1m2f Listed winner here in 2023 but well beaten on his only visit since; no win since a Group 3 in August 2023 but placed in AW/turf handicaps (1m-1m4f) this spring and continues down the weights; needs much better than he produced on last two outings.

RaceName: Betfred Ladies' Derby Handicap (For Female Amateur Jockeys) (GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: Did well on AW last winter and even better form when the cheekpieces replaced a visor at Goodwood (1m4f, good) four weeks ago, leading over 1f out to score with rather more authority than the half-length winning margin suggests; used to act on good to firm; up 3lb but firmly in the picture.

RaceName: Betfred Ladies' Derby Handicap (For Female Amateur Jockeys) (GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: 8yo who went 21 months without a win but has now scored at Windsor, Lingfield, Chepstow and Ripon in his four races this turf season; his second in this race last year was a remote one and the 6lb rise since his latest start asks a question, but conditions fine and he's on a major roll, ridden by Sarah Bowen on last two starts (two in four days); major player.

RaceName: Betfred Ladies' Derby Handicap (For Female Amateur Jockeys) (GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: Bumper and 1m4f AW handicap winner; only three runs on Flat turf, the latest when second of 13 at Windsor (1m2f, good to firm) in May in first-time cheekpieces, which have been kept since; not disgraced when Georgia King was on again back on AW latest outing; needs a second look.

RaceName: Betfred Ladies' Derby Handicap (For Female Amateur Jockeys) (GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: Creditable third off 9lb higher over C&D on soft ground (acts on good to firm) last September in his only Epsom race; AW campaign in the winter for new yard did not go at all well until he perked up a bit in February on his last two outings; needs to build on that after his break but he's too well handicapped to be ignored.

RaceName: Betfred Ladies' Derby Handicap (For Female Amateur Jockeys) (GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: Sole win in AW maiden as 3yo; that's 0-15 since and although he was involved in some tight finishes on AW early in 2026, his second of three to Booziebrunch (runs 6.55 today) over 1m2f at Newmarket three weeks ago was a remote one; perhaps he needed that after three months off but he does not look the percentage call and has a slight question at 1m4f.

RaceName: CopyBet Daily Profit Boost Novices' Hurdle (GBB Race)
OwnerName: James Paul Services To Industry Ltd
Spotlight: On his third trainer and has rather chequered form but he won on hurdling debut at Sedgefield (2m1f, good) in May and ran to a similar level when runner-up under a penalty at Kelso later that month; struggled back on the Flat last time but that was in the Northumberland Vase and he's respected back in this sphere; tongue-tie and cheekpieces are added.

RaceName: CopyBet Daily Profit Boost Novices' Hurdle (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Useful Flat performer who made it third-time lucky over hurdles with a dominant display after wind surgery at Stratford (2m2f, good to firm) two weeks ago; just about sets the standard under a penalty and he could have a lot more to offer in this sphere; strong contender.

RaceName: CopyBet Daily Profit Boost Novices' Hurdle (GBB Race)
Spotlight: 0-7 in Irish points and he's been tailed off at big prices in both hurdle runs including behind Square d'Alboni at Stratford two weeks ago.

RaceName: CopyBet Daily Profit Boost Novices' Hurdle (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Only 1-12 in points and he's been beaten 44l and 43l in two hurdle runs (2m, good) this summer.

RaceName: CopyBet Daily Profit Boost Novices' Hurdle (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Point winner in April but he has a modest completion rate in that sphere and was pulled up at Kingston Blount last time; now makes his rules debut and he's probably best watched.

RaceName: CopyBet Daily Profit Boost Novices' Hurdle (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Just 1-18 on the Flat but showed fair form when placed twice over 1m2f this spring; should have a future over hurdles but he made a low-key start in this sphere over C&D (good; 20-1) last month; stablemate of Chemistry.

RaceName: CopyBet Daily Profit Boost Novices' Hurdle (GBB Race)
OwnerName: H Maye And The Joe Tickle Racing Club
Spotlight: Poor form on the Flat and was pulled up in his only previous run over hurdles; can only be watched back in this sphere.

RaceName: CopyBet Daily Profit Boost Novices' Hurdle (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Beaten out of sight in her sole bumper and same story at massive prices in two hurdle runs this summer.

RaceName: Mervue Equine Handicap
Spotlight: Three Flat wins for this yard, including over 1m6f and 2m; unplaced in five starts on the level since winning over 10.5f on AW in late 2024, but was placed in a maiden hurdle in February; has gone to Galway the last three years, and this is most likely a prep run.

RaceName: Mervue Equine Handicap
Spotlight: AW winner surprised his trainer when handling soft ground here last month, beating Monday's ready Killarney winner Molto Amichi over 1m1f in first-time cheekpieces; winner got first run on him at Ballinrobe over almost this far, where he kept on well along with third-home Marmeladova.

RaceName: Mervue Equine Handicap
Spotlight: Loves Dundalk and won three in a row there in the spring (10.5f/1m4f); sole turf win came here last summer over 1m1f (off 60) and will be sharper for Roscommon run after nearly three months off; AW mark of 87; turf mark of 78 reflects his good AW form earlier in the year, rather than his actual achievements on grass.

RaceName: Mervue Equine Handicap
Spotlight: Ballinrobe maiden winner was a length off Daler back there last month (now 1lb better off); seemingly outstayed over 1m4f last time; touched off at Navan last year, so she goes left-handed; handicapped to win when everything falls into place.

RaceName: Mervue Equine Handicap
Spotlight: Gained her second AW win off 72 in February, but just 1-28 on turf; good fourth of 20 over 1m at the Curragh last time; best form over 7f/1m, and just one inconclusive run over this trip here last year; yet to finish in the first three in five visits to this venue.

RaceName: Mervue Equine Handicap
OwnerName: Mrs Helen Butler & John A Griffin & Mrs Bernadette Fahey
Spotlight: Sole win in 26 Flat starts was over 1m1f in lower-grade contest here last August; that is possibly his optimum trip, although only beaten half-a-length over 10.5f on AW in December; after five months off, not a bad run at Roscommon lately.

RaceName: Mervue Equine Handicap
Spotlight: Both wins have come right-handed on yielding ground, over 1m3f last year and under a brilliant ride from the champion jockey over 9.5f at Gowran last month; has run well this way round, too; handled quick ground okay when ridden to get 1m4f (upped to this grade) at the Curragh last time; cheekpieces swapped for blinkers.

RaceName: Mervue Equine Handicap
Spotlight: Veteran front-runner won over 1m4f at Roscommon last year and was second at Fairyhouse in May; all eight wins have been at right-handed tracks; has to be opposed here.

RaceName: Mervue Equine Handicap
Spotlight: Came good late in his three-year-old campaign when the blinds went on; back-to-back wins in 0-60 company at Gowran (1m, heavy) and  Dundalk (10.5f); after eight months off, ran okay when upgraded to this level, finishing pretty much upsides Unterberg and Sinbad My Dad at Roscommon (10.5f).

RaceName: Matchbook Exchange INH Flat Race
Spotlight: One of two runners for yard; second foal; dam placed 2m2f-3m1f hurdles, sister to winner Montys Meadow (useful bumper/2m hurdle/2m4f chase) and family of Grand National winner Monty's Pass; decent pedigree but a E5,000 vendor buyback as a foal; worth a market check; tongue-tie.

RaceName: Matchbook Exchange INH Flat Race
Spotlight: First foal; dam point winner, half-sister to 2m3f-3m3f hurdle winner Bryden Boy and bumper winner Hideaway Vic, out of bumper/2m4f hurdle winner; E10,000 foal is by a quality French sire and his yard does well at this track, so worth a market check.

RaceName: Matchbook Exchange INH Flat Race
Spotlight: Solid debut effort at Roscommon on yielding to soft ground, but didn't improve from that at Limerick since, albeit still a decent effort; sets the standard on form, but vulnerable to a decent newcomer.

RaceName: Matchbook Exchange INH Flat Race
Spotlight: Second runner for yard; raced too keenly when beaten 28l in a Down Royal bumper in May; will need to settle better and a hood may help.

RaceName: Matchbook Exchange INH Flat Race
Spotlight: Fourth foal; half-brother to point winner Diamond Jetaway; dam placed 2m3f-3m hurdles, sister to useful 2m-3m hurdle/chase winner Jet Master, from family of high-class chaser Blazing Walker; unsold for E4,800 as a 3yo; yard can ready a newcomer, so needs a market check.

RaceName: Matchbook Exchange INH Flat Race
Spotlight: Ninth foal; half-brother to AW bumper and useful 2m-3m hurdle/chase winner Golden Taipan; dam unraced and from the family of high-class chaser The Grey Monk; by a promising young sire and cost E22,000 as a 3yo; tongue-tie on for debut; top rider booked and one to be interested in, especially if there is market strength.

RaceName: Matchbook Exchange INH Flat Race
Spotlight: First foal; dam 1m2f winner (inc AW; RPR 82), sister to 1m4f-2m winner Pantomime, half-sister to 7f/1m winner Darkening, out of 6f winner; Flat-bred but there is stamina in his damline; could run respectably on debut.

RaceName: Matchbook Exchange INH Flat Race
OwnerName: Exors of the Late Michael Moloney
Spotlight: Second foal; brother to point winner Fox Walk; dam 1m7f hurdle winner, out of 2m hurdle-winning half-sister to Grand National winner Rule The World; by the champion sire but cost only E16,000 as a 3yo; top rider booked and one to check for market strength.

RaceName: Vail Williams Handicap
Spotlight: Made all last term in a maiden at Brighton (good to firm) and a handicap over C&D (good to soft), the latter in September on final run for Andrew Balding; back over C&D on last two outings and not disgraced when sixth of ten on latest (co-favourite); case can be made back on last winning mark.

RaceName: Vail Williams Handicap
Spotlight: Won at Newmarket (7f, good) in May, ran respectably at Goodwood next time and beaten a neck at Newmarket four weeks ago; finished last in April (reappearance) in his only race on good to firm but otherwise needs plenty of respect.

RaceName: Vail Williams Handicap
Spotlight: C&D winner in 2024 but three subsequent visits have not gone nearly so well; none too consistent but two of his last four AW runs, including in January latest, give him a shout if he's ready following the absence; he's twice been runner-up after longer layoffs.

RaceName: Vail Williams Handicap
Spotlight: Reliable sort (on turf and AW) who won twice last summer and was runner-up in March/April on his last two outings; stable has struck often enough this summer for him to be given a close look despite this new track.

RaceName: Vail Williams Handicap
Spotlight: Can make the running and he went on before halfway when gamely winning a six-runnner handicap at Catterick (7f, good to soft) five weeks ago, getting off the mark at eighth attempt; back up 2lb and unraced on good to firm, with this track a new experience too, but that latest run gives him a shot.

RaceName: CopyBet Follow Us On X Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: 5yo who made it 2-7 over hurdles when scoring in a first-time visor at Newton Abbot (3m2f, good) last month; that was by a nose but he hit a personal best with that success and is still unexposed at staying trips; respected up 4lb.

RaceName: CopyBet Follow Us On X Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Runner-up on handicap debut here (2m4f, good) last July but he's struggled since, including in three runs this year; overall record is now 0-10 and he has plenty to prove back up in trip.

RaceName: CopyBet Follow Us On X Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Won off 10lb higher at Huntingdon (2m3f, good) last May but he's been disappointing since and was beaten 10l back on the Flat (1m6f) two weeks ago; has lots to prove and this is his first run beyond 2m5f.

RaceName: CopyBet Follow Us On X Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Her sole win was over fences in France in January 2024; has dropped a long way in the weights and she's been tailed off in last two runs and is now 0-10 for current yard; opposable.

RaceName: CopyBet Follow Us On X Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Pulled up in his final run during winter but that was in the mud and he won by 7l over this trip at Exeter (good to soft) in December; looks interesting on that form and he needs a close look back from five months off.

RaceName: CopyBet Follow Us On X Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: 0-7 under rules but he's reached the frame in both runs for his current yard including when third in a C&D handicap (good) last week; on same mark here and has each-way claims again.

RaceName: CopyBet Follow Us On X Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Won at Market Rasen (2m4f, good) in June 2024 but he's not seen much action since and has struggled over hurdles and fences in last five runs; now 14lb lower than for his win but he needs a major revival back in this sphere.

RaceName: BOYLE Sports Home Of The Early Payout Handicap
Spotlight: Both wins in August, so coming into her time of year; money for her when fourth over 1m2f here in May, and stayed on from rear when fifth in the Ladies Derby over 1m2f at the Curragh, a race won by well-backed stablemate; that run might have sweetened her up and she can reverse placings with third-placed Cooley's Mist; hampered when fourth to Tatum in last year's renewal; has won over 1m4f.

RaceName: BOYLE Sports Home Of The Early Payout Handicap
Spotlight: Won over 1m at Gowran last summer, and twice in the autumn over 10.5f at Dundalk; return over 1m4f was respectable; slow to stride and weakened tamely over 1m2f at Navan on Saturday; tongue-tie and blinkers worn then replaced by the cheekpieces worn for AW wins; stamina on dam's side of pedigree, but not sure to stay this far.

RaceName: BOYLE Sports Home Of The Early Payout Handicap
Spotlight: Placed over 1m4f on AW and, after five months off, wasn't stopping when 20-1 winner of first  handicap at Naas (1m3f, good); by a Derby winner and dam (by Dubawi) won over 10.5f; should stay and obvious chance.

RaceName: BOYLE Sports Home Of The Early Payout Handicap
Spotlight: Latest of four wins was over 1m4f three years ago, but he's on a good mark nowadays and came home well when around 3l fifth to Killyclogher Rose at Naas (1m3f); she's a lot less exposed though, and he might struggle to turn around that form.

RaceName: BOYLE Sports Home Of The Early Payout Handicap
Spotlight: Placed at two before missing 3yo season; two wins here last year, including corresponding contest (Ob La Di well-held fourth); not seen after that until fair comeback over 1m2f here in May, and 2m1f may have stretched her at Limerick; only 4lb higher than last year; likes it good, but scratched because of quick ground last time.

RaceName: BOYLE Sports Home Of The Early Payout Handicap
Spotlight: Lightly-raced 4-y-o; fourth in big-field handicap over 1m1f here last summer when with Natalia Lupini; placed twice last month, again over 1m1f here and when third in Ladies Derby at the Curragh,  a length in front of Ob La Di; stamina is the concern (not bred to stay this far).

RaceName: Kate Kearney's Cottage (Ladies Pro/Am) INH Flat Race
Spotlight: Eighth foal; dam placed 2m4f/2m6f hurdles, half-sister to 7f Group 3 winner Marvada (dam of 1m2f Group 2 winner Terebellum), out of French 1m3f winner; dam hasn't a great record at stud; unsold for E1,800 as a 3yo; doesn't make a lot of appeal on debut.

RaceName: Kate Kearney's Cottage (Ladies Pro/Am) INH Flat Race
Spotlight: Beaten 18l on sole completed point start and doesn't make much appeal on bumper debut.

RaceName: Kate Kearney's Cottage (Ladies Pro/Am) INH Flat Race
Spotlight: Beaten 23l on debut at Punchestown and will have to improve plenty on that to score.

RaceName: Kate Kearney's Cottage (Ladies Pro/Am) INH Flat Race
Spotlight: Half-brother to five winners including the useful Farceur Du Large; started 5/2 favourite and was running respectably on point debut when falling last year; likely best watched unless strong in the market.

RaceName: Kate Kearney's Cottage (Ladies Pro/Am) INH Flat Race
Spotlight: Beaten 17l and 23l in two bumpers on varying ground conditions; fitted with a hood and tongue-tie today, but best watched for the time being.

RaceName: Kate Kearney's Cottage (Ladies Pro/Am) INH Flat Race
Spotlight: Not a lot of promise when failing to complete in two point starts; however, a different story over C&D on bumper debut when beaten only 11l; the tongue-tie worn then is retained and should have improved since, so is a player.

RaceName: Kate Kearney's Cottage (Ladies Pro/Am) INH Flat Race
OwnerName: John F Doyle & R Bannerman & Mrs Sheila O'Ryan
Spotlight: Well beaten in the Goffs Defender bumper on debut and not much better in a Limerick maiden hurdle; best watched for the time being.

RaceName: Kate Kearney's Cottage (Ladies Pro/Am) INH Flat Race
Spotlight: Beaten 9l on sole point start by a rival that made E100,000 since; ran a cracker on bumper debut at Downpatrick when runner-up to a more experienced rival; can improve for that and a leading lady rider aboard, so a solid chance.

RaceName: Kate Kearney's Cottage (Ladies Pro/Am) INH Flat Race
Spotlight: Second foal; dam runner-up in 1m3f/1m4f AQPS Flat races and 2m4f hurdle, half-sister to high-class bumper and 2m-2m7f/chase hurdle winner Bacardys; E25,000 foal then made only E16,000 as a 3yo; decent pedigree; tongue-tie for debut; best watched unless market support.

RaceName: Kate Kearney's Cottage (Ladies Pro/Am) INH Flat Race
Spotlight: Third foal; half-brother to bumper winner Wilton Mills Trier; dam unraced half-sister to winners Solsbury Hill (2m hurdle/chase) and Reelingintheyears (2m3f hurdle); E5,000 foal was unsold for E4,500 as a 3yo; not a bad pedigree and could be capable of running well.

RaceName: Kate Kearney's Cottage (Ladies Pro/Am) INH Flat Race
Spotlight: Half-sister to Champion Bumper winner Bambino Fever; was weak in the market when beaten 13l on debut at Ballinrobe last September; likely to do better after an absence and is one for the shortlist.

RaceName: Kate Kearney's Cottage (Ladies Pro/Am) INH Flat Race
Spotlight: Ninth foal; half-sister to winners Thou Art Peter (1m/1m2f inc HK dirt; RPR 80) and Bungle Inthedesert (1m AW; 73); dam unraced half-sister to useful 1m2f winner Saab Alamanal; Flat-bred filly was a E5,000 vendor buyback as a yearling; stamina concerns, so best watched for future reference.

RaceName: Kate Kearney's Cottage (Ladies Pro/Am) INH Flat Race
Spotlight: By a useful sire and from the family of Gold Cup winner Long Run; however, has been pulled-up on both point runs, so isn't easy to fancy; tongue-tie on.

RaceName: Kate Kearney's Cottage (Ladies Pro/Am) INH Flat Race
Spotlight: Respectable debut effort for former yard when beaten 16l in a Galway bumper nearly a year ago; absence since is a concern, but could run well for a respected local yard.

RaceName: Kate Kearney's Cottage (Ladies Pro/Am) INH Flat Race
Spotlight: Sister to 2m5f-3m2f chase winner Village Master, half-sister to four winners including Bun Doran (high-class bumper and 2m-2m4f hurdle/chase) and Shantou Village (smart bumper and 2m4f-2m7f hurdle/chase); a sibling to some smart sorts, but seems the champion trainer's second-string; could still run well; tongue-tie.

RaceName: Kate Kearney's Cottage (Ladies Pro/Am) INH Flat Race
Spotlight: Ninth foal; half-sister to winners Slainte Mhor (3m chase) and Persian Daughter (2m6f hurdle); dam unraced half-sister to high-class 2m5f-3m6f hurdle/chase winner Ad Hoc; by a useful sire and a decent damline; cost E4,000 as a 3yo; will likely improve from this.

RaceName: Betfred Everswift 30 July Handicap
Spotlight: Unraced on good to firm except on debut (last of four); yard is among the winners and a recent, never-nearer fifth of 12 on the Kempton AW (7f; 12-1) was a respectable start to this filly's 3yo campaign, not getting the clearest of runs late on; needs a close look.

RaceName: Betfred Everswift 30 July Handicap
Spotlight: Probably started handicap life on a demanding mark and he'd dropped 10lb before going down by just under 2l when fifth of 15 at Doncaster (7f, good) in first-time blinkers on latest outing; hit the front over 1f out that day but wandered under pressure; needs to see it out better.

RaceName: Betfred Everswift 30 July Handicap
Spotlight: Nine-race maiden; reappearance was his best show this term and, having had four starts since, it's hard to get excited about his chance today despite the falling mark; first wore a visor on latest outing.

RaceName: Betfred Everswift 30 July Handicap
Spotlight: Best form when fourth of 13 on handicap debut at Windsor (6f, good to firm), faring best of those held up; readily outgunned once in the clear at Haydock (upped to 7f) later in May but soft ground was a possible excuse.

RaceName: Betfred Everswift 30 July Handicap
Spotlight: Best effort in eight starts for Richard Hannon when beaten a neck in a Wolverhampton nursery (8.6f, AW) in December; 6,000gns buy in February before never dangerous in a small field at Brighton (7f, good to firm; 28-1) 16 days ago; needs a rapid revival.

RaceName: Betfred Everswift 30 July Handicap
Spotlight: Sole win from 17 races was on Kempton AW (6f) in January; new yard when back on turf for last three outings, not far away in two of those but better is needed to win this; his only two attempts over this far did not convince.

RaceName: Betfred Everswift 30 July Handicap
Spotlight: Eight-race maiden who made the running on first three starts this term but switched to trying to come from the rear when hooded on both since; Newmarket latest (7f, good to firm; 40-1) was a positive performance as he seemed to be gathering momentum in the closing stages and was beaten just 2l in sixth of 11; dropped 2lb today and everything may well click into place soon.

RaceName: Betfred Everswift 30 July Handicap
Spotlight: Debut (well beaten) was her only start on turf; sent handicapping for last two outings and came second of eight at Wolverhampton (7f) in February on latest, albeit beaten 13l; relatively early days but it's not yet clear that she's worth this mark.

RaceName: CopyBet Overnight Best Odds Guaranteed Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Won four of his first five for Olly Murphy (including a handicap off much higher mark) but he's lost his way badly.

RaceName: CopyBet Overnight Best Odds Guaranteed Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Breakthrough success in 2m4f handicap at Ffos Las and off this 2lb higher mark she was in contention when brought down three out here a month ago (Shesonnet runner-up); respected.

RaceName: CopyBet Overnight Best Odds Guaranteed Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Warwick winner in May (2m5f, good) and kept on to go down by only a length over C&D a month ago; solid chance off 2lb higher.

RaceName: CopyBet Overnight Best Odds Guaranteed Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Out of form in two runs last winter but she is 3-12 over hurdles and realistically handicapped if she can rediscover her form; market could be helpful back from a break.

RaceName: CopyBet Overnight Best Odds Guaranteed Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Exploited much-reduced mark at Southwell (2m4f, good) and it was over 2m7f when not so good at Bangor; this should be more her trip but she is a hard mare to predict.

RaceName: CopyBet Overnight Best Odds Guaranteed Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Useful bumper winner for Ben Brookhouse; still lightly raced and runner-up in both his handicaps, only narrowly failing to justify market support last time here over 2m; only 3lb higher and should stay this far; has been held up in recent races.

RaceName: CopyBet Overnight Best Odds Guaranteed Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Won a 2m4f Perth handicap for Ewan Whillans in April (good) when 4lb lower; tailed off in two subsequent runs, the latest when only 3-1 for yard debut at Stratford (2m; reportedly never travelled); clearly capable of a big run but risky at the minute.

RaceName: CopyBet Overnight Best Odds Guaranteed Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: The last of his four hurdle wins was in late 2023; well beaten in this discipline before pulling up on last week's belated return to fences.

RaceName: CopyBet Overnight Best Odds Guaranteed Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Winning pointer; 0-14 over hurdles but has placed form and interesting that he was sent off the 7-2 favourite when pretty much tailed off here 15 days ago (after three months off); hard to trust on the back of that effort, for all that better was presumably expected.
